text: The proverbs of Solomon, the son of David, king of Israel:
summary: This is an introduction stating the source of the proverbs, not a standalone piece of wisdom.

text:
to know wisdom and instruction;
to discern the words of understanding;
summary: The purpose of this teaching is to gain wisdom, learn discipline, and understand insightful guidance.

text:
to receive instruction in wise dealing,
in righteousness, justice, and equity;
summary: Learn to handle situations wisely by practicing fairness, justice, and integrity.

text:
to give prudence to the simple,
knowledge and discretion to the young man:
summary: Teaching simple and young people prudence, knowledge, and discretion helps them make wise decisions.

text:
that the wise man may hear, and increase in learning;
that the man of understanding may attain to sound counsel:
summary: A truly wise person listens and continually seeks to grow in knowledge and sound judgment.

text:
to understand a proverb, and parables,
the words and riddles of the wise.
summary: Learning to interpret proverbs, parables, and wise sayings helps deepen understanding and insight.

text:
The fear of Yahweh is the beginning of knowledge;
but the foolish despise wisdom and instruction.
summary: Respecting God is the foundation of true knowledge, while rejecting wisdom and guidance is foolish.

text:
My son, listen to your father’s instruction,
and don’t forsake your mother’s teaching:
summary: Value and follow the guidance and teachings of your parents for a wise and disciplined life.

text:
for they will be a garland to grace your head,
and chains around your neck.
summary: Valuable teachings and guidance will adorn and honor you like beautiful jewelry.

text:
My son, if sinners entice you,
don’t consent.
summary: Don't give in to peer pressure when it leads you to do wrong.

text:
If they say, “Come with us.
Let’s lay in wait for blood.
Let’s lurk secretly for the innocent without cause.
summary: Avoid joining those who plan harm or injustice against innocent people.

text:
Let’s swallow them up alive like Sheol,
and whole, like those who go down into the pit.
summary: This proverb uses vivid imagery of destruction to warn against harmful associations, but its meaning is harsh and context-specific.

text:
We’ll find all valuable wealth.
We’ll fill our houses with plunder.
summary: This proverb reflects the temptation to gain wealth through dishonest means, which is not a wise or ethical approach.

text:
You shall cast your lot among us.
We’ll all have one purse.”
summary: Joining with others to share resources can create a sense of unity and common purpose.

text:
My son, don’t walk on the path with them.
Keep your foot from their path,
summary: Avoid following the wrong crowd to keep yourself on a good path.

text:
for their feet run to evil.
They hurry to shed blood.
summary: People who rush into wrongdoing and violence lack self-control and discipline.

text: For in vain is the net spread in the sight of any bird:
summary: Trying to trap someone who is aware of the danger is pointless.

text:
but these lay wait for their own blood.
They lurk secretly for their own lives.
summary: Those who plot harm against others ultimately bring harm upon themselves.

text:
So are the ways of everyone who is greedy for gain.
It takes away the life of its owners.
summary: Greed for quick gain ultimately destroys those who pursue it.

text:
Wisdom calls aloud in the street.
She utters her voice in the public squares.
summary: Wisdom is openly available and calls out for everyone to listen in everyday life.

text:
She calls at the head of noisy places.
At the entrance of the city gates, she utters her words:
summary: Wisdom publicly calls out in busy, central places to be heard by all.

text:
“How long, you simple ones, will you love simplicity?
How long will mockers delight themselves in mockery,
and fools hate knowledge?
summary: How long will you keep rejecting wisdom and choose to remain ignorant and foolish?

text:
Turn at my reproof.
Behold, I will pour out my spirit on you.
I will make known my words to you.
summary: Be open to correction and guidance, and you will receive insight and understanding.

text:
Because I have called, and you have refused;
I have stretched out my hand, and no one has paid attention;
summary: Wisdom calls out to people, but some choose to ignore it.

text:
but you have ignored all my counsel,
and wanted none of my reproof;
summary: Ignoring wise advice and correction leads to missed opportunities for growth.

text:
I also will laugh at your disaster.
I will mock when calamity overtakes you;
summary: Ignoring wisdom leads to consequences that may seem harsh or mocking in hindsight.
Trouble and hardship will come unexpectedly like a storm or whirlwind.

text:
Then will they call on me, but I will not answer.
They will seek me diligently, but they will not find me;
summary: If you ignore wisdom and discipline now, you may later seek help in vain when consequences come.

text:
because they hated knowledge,
and didn’t choose the fear of Yahweh.
summary: Rejecting knowledge and the reverence of God leads to negative consequences.

text:
They wanted none of my counsel.
They despised all my reproof.
summary: People often reject wise advice and correction, which can lead to negative consequences.

text:
Therefore they will eat of the fruit of their own way,
and be filled with their own schemes.
summary: People will face the consequences of their own choices and plans.

text:
For the backsliding of the simple will kill them.
The careless ease of fools will destroy them.
summary: Neglecting wisdom and living carelessly leads to self-destruction.

text:
But whoever listens to me will dwell securely,
and will be at ease, without fear of harm.”
summary: Those who heed wise counsel will live safely and without fear.

text:
My son, if you will receive my words,
and store up my commandments within you;
summary: If you eagerly accept and remember wise teachings, you will gain valuable understanding.

text:
So as to turn your ear to wisdom,
and apply your heart to understanding;
summary: Focus your attention and commit your heart to gaining wisdom and understanding.

text:
Yes, if you call out for discernment,
and lift up your voice for understanding;
summary: Seek discernment and understanding earnestly and persistently.

text:
If you seek her as silver,
and search for her as for hidden treasures:
summary: Pursue wisdom with the same eagerness and value as you would seek precious silver or hidden treasures.

text:
then you will understand the fear of Yahweh,
and find the knowledge of God.
summary: By seeking understanding, you will come to respect God and gain true knowledge.

text:
For Yahweh gives wisdom.
Out of his mouth comes knowledge and understanding.
summary: True wisdom and understanding come from God.

text:
He lays up sound wisdom for the upright.
He is a shield to those who walk in integrity;
summary: God provides true wisdom and protection to those who live with integrity.

text:
that he may guard the paths of justice,
and preserve the way of his saints.
summary: God protects those who live justly and follow His ways.

text:
Then you will understand righteousness and justice,
equity and every good path.
summary: Pursuing wisdom leads to understanding what is right, just, fair, and good in life.

text:
For wisdom will enter into your heart.
Knowledge will be pleasant to your soul.
summary: Embracing wisdom enriches your inner self and brings joy to your soul.

text:
Discretion will watch over you.
Understanding will keep you,
summary: Good judgment and understanding protect and guide you through life's challenges.

text:
to deliver you from the way of evil,
from the men who speak perverse things;
summary: Wisdom protects you from harmful influences and deceptive people.

text:
who forsake the paths of uprightness,
to walk in the ways of darkness;
summary: Turning away from doing what is right leads one to follow harmful and destructive ways.

text:
who rejoice to do evil,
and delight in the perverseness of evil;
summary: Some people take pleasure in doing wrong and enjoy corrupt behavior.

text:
who are crooked in their ways,
and wayward in their paths:
summary: This describes people who are dishonest and stray from the right path.

text:
To deliver you from the strange woman,
even from the foreigner who flatters with her words;
summary: Avoid being misled by flattering strangers who may lead you into harmful situations.

text:
who forsakes the friend of her youth,
and forgets the covenant of her God:
summary: Turning away from long-standing friendships and commitments to God breaks important bonds and trust.

text:
for her house leads down to death,
her paths to the departed spirits.
summary: Following destructive influences ultimately leads to ruin and separation from life.

text:
None who go to her return again,
neither do they attain to the paths of life:
summary: Those who follow destructive paths do not find life or return to the right way.

text:
that you may walk in the way of good men,
and keep the paths of the righteous.
summary: Follow the example of good and righteous people to live a virtuous life.

text:
For the upright will dwell in the land.
The perfect will remain in it.
summary: Those who live with integrity and moral uprightness will find lasting stability and security in their lives.

text:
But the wicked will be cut off from the land.
The treacherous will be rooted out of it.
summary: Those who act wickedly or betray others will ultimately be removed from their place or position.

text:
My son, don’t forget my teaching;
but let your heart keep my commandments:
summary: Remember and cherish wise teachings by keeping them close to your heart.

text:
for length of days, and years of life,
and peace, will they add to you.
summary: Following wise guidance brings a longer life and peace.

text:
Don’t let kindness and truth forsake you.
Bind them around your neck.
Write them on the tablet of your heart.
summary: Always hold kindness and truth close to you and make them central to your character.

text:
So you will find favor,
and good understanding in the sight of God and man.
summary: Living with integrity leads to gaining respect and favor from both God and people.

text:
Trust in Yahweh with all your heart,
and don’t lean on your own understanding.
summary: Fully trust in God rather than relying solely on your own insight.

text:
In all your ways acknowledge him,
and he will make your paths straight.
summary: Trust and honor God in everything you do, and He will guide you on the right path.

text:
Don’t be wise in your own eyes.
Fear Yahweh, and depart from evil.
summary: Avoid arrogance by recognizing your limitations, respect God, and turn away from wrongdoing.

text:
It will be health to your body,
and nourishment to your bones.
summary: Following wise guidance promotes physical well-being and strength.

text:
Honor Yahweh with your substance,
with the first fruits of all your increase:
summary: Respect and honor God by giving back the best and first portion of your earnings.

text:
so your barns will be filled with plenty,
and your vats will overflow with new wine.
summary: When you are faithful and diligent, your resources and blessings will increase abundantly.

text:
My son, don’t despise Yahweh’s discipline,
neither be weary of his reproof:
summary: Don't resent or grow tired of God's correction, as it is meant to guide and improve you.

text:
for whom Yahweh loves, he reproves;
even as a father reproves the son in whom he delights.
summary: God's loving correction is like a father's guidance to a child he cherishes, meant to help us grow and improve.

text:
Happy is the man who finds wisdom,
the man who gets understanding.
summary: Finding wisdom and understanding brings true happiness and fulfillment.

text:
For her good profit is better than getting silver,
and her return is better than fine gold.
summary: True value and profit come from wisdom, which surpasses material riches like silver and gold.

text:
She is more precious than rubies.
None of the things you can desire are to be compared to her.
summary: True wisdom is more valuable than any material wealth or desire.

text:
Length of days is in her right hand.
In her left hand are riches and honor.
summary: True wisdom brings both a long life and material blessings along with respect.

text:
Her ways are ways of pleasantness.
All her paths are peace.
summary: Following wisdom leads to a life filled with peace and pleasantness.

text:
She is a tree of life to those who lay hold of her.
Happy is everyone who retains her.
summary: Wisdom is like a life-giving tree that brings happiness to those who embrace and hold onto it.

text:
By wisdom Yahweh founded the earth.
By understanding, he established the heavens.
summary: The world is created and sustained through divine wisdom and understanding, highlighting the power and importance of wisdom in creation.

text:
By his knowledge, the depths were broken up,
and the skies drop down the dew.
summary: Understanding and knowledge reveal the workings of nature and bring about life-giving blessings.

text:
My son, let them not depart from your eyes.
Keep sound wisdom and discretion:
summary: Always hold onto sound wisdom and good judgment as guiding principles in life.

text:
so they will be life to your soul,
and grace for your neck.
summary: Embracing wisdom brings vitality and honor to your life.

text:
Then you shall walk in your way securely.
Your foot won’t stumble.
summary: Living wisely leads to confidence and stability in your life's journey.

text:
When you lie down, you will not be afraid.
Yes, you will lie down, and your sleep will be sweet.
summary: When you trust and live wisely, you can rest without fear and enjoy peaceful sleep.

text:
Don’t be afraid of sudden fear,
neither of the desolation of the wicked, when it comes:
summary: Don't let sudden fear or the downfall of the wicked cause you to be afraid.

text:
for Yahweh will be your confidence,
and will keep your foot from being taken.
summary: Trusting in God provides security and stability in life.

text:
Don’t withhold good from those to whom it is due,
when it is in the power of your hand to do it.
summary: Always do good to others when you have the ability and opportunity to help them.
If you have what someone needs, don't delay helping them—be prompt and trustworthy in your actions.

text:
Don’t devise evil against your neighbor,
since he dwells securely by you.
summary: Don't plot harm against those who live near you, as it disrupts community trust and peace.

text:
Don’t strive with a man without cause,
if he has done you no harm.
summary: Avoid unnecessary conflicts with others when they haven't wronged you.

text:
Don’t envy the man of violence.
Choose none of his ways.
summary: Avoid envying violent people and refuse to follow their harmful behaviors.

text:
For the perverse is an abomination to Yahweh,
but his friendship is with the upright.
summary: God values integrity and rejects those who are morally crooked, favoring those who live uprightly.

text:
Yahweh’s curse is in the house of the wicked,
but he blesses the habitation of the righteous.
summary: Evil actions bring misfortune, while living righteously invites blessings and peace.

text:
Surely he mocks the mockers,
but he gives grace to the humble.
summary: Those who are proud face scorn, but those who are humble receive kindness and favor.

text:
The wise will inherit glory,
but shame will be the promotion of fools.
summary: Those who are wise gain honor, while fools bring themselves disgrace.

text:
Listen, sons, to a father’s instruction.
Pay attention and know understanding;
summary: Pay close attention to the guidance of those with experience to gain true understanding.

text:
for I give you sound learning.
Don’t forsake my law.
summary: Embrace and hold firmly to wise teachings and guidance for a sound life.

text:
For I was a son to my father,
tender and an only child in the sight of my mother.
summary: The speaker reflects on their cherished position as a beloved child to their parents.

text:
He taught me, and said to me:
“Let your heart retain my words.
Keep my commandments, and live.
summary: Hold onto wise teachings and follow instructions to live a fulfilling life.

text:
Get wisdom.
Get understanding.
Don’t forget, and don’t deviate from the words of my mouth.
summary: Pursue wisdom and understanding diligently, and stay true to sound guidance.

text:
Don’t forsake her, and she will preserve you.
Love her, and she will keep you.
summary: Hold on to wisdom and cherish it, and it will protect and guide you throughout life.

text:
Wisdom is supreme.
Get wisdom.
Yes, though it costs all your possessions, get understanding.
summary: Pursue wisdom above all else, even if it requires great sacrifice.

text:
Esteem her, and she will exalt you.
She will bring you to honor, when you embrace her.
summary: Value wisdom highly, and it will elevate and honor you in life.

text:
She will give to your head a garland of grace.
She will deliver a crown of splendor to you.”
summary: Wisdom brings honor and beauty to your life, like a graceful crown on your head.

text:
Listen, my son, and receive my sayings.
The years of your life will be many.
summary: Pay attention to wise advice to enjoy a long and fulfilling life.

text:
I have taught you in the way of wisdom.
I have led you in straight paths.
summary: Guidance in life should be rooted in wisdom, leading one on a clear and righteous path.

text:
When you go, your steps will not be hampered.
When you run, you will not stumble.
summary: If you follow wise guidance, your path will be smooth and you will avoid pitfalls.

text:
Take firm hold of instruction.
Don’t let her go.
Keep her, for she is your life.
summary: Hold tightly to guidance and teaching, as they are essential for a meaningful life.

text:
Don’t enter into the path of the wicked.
Don’t walk in the way of evil men.
summary: Avoid associating with or following the ways of wicked people to protect your integrity and well-being.

text:
Avoid it, and don’t pass by it.
Turn from it, and pass on.
summary: Stay away from harmful paths and deliberately choose to avoid them.

text:
For they don’t sleep, unless they do evil.
Their sleep is taken away, unless they make someone fall.
summary: People who plot harm or wrongdoing are restless and troubled, unable to find peace.

text:
For they eat the bread of wickedness,
and drink the wine of violence.
summary: Those who follow wicked ways indulge in harmful and violent behaviors.

text:
But the path of the righteous is like the dawning light,
that shines more and more until the perfect day.
summary: The journey of a righteous person is like increasing daylight, growing clearer and better over time.

text:
The way of the wicked is like darkness.
They don’t know what they stumble over.
summary: Those who live wickedly lack clear direction and often face unforeseen troubles.

text:
My son, attend to my words.
Turn your ear to my sayings.
summary: Pay close attention and listen carefully to wise advice.

text:
Let them not depart from your eyes.
Keep them in the center of your heart.
summary: Keep wise teachings always in your sight and in your heart to guide your life.

text:
For they are life to those who find them,
and health to their whole body.
summary: Wisdom brings vitality and well-being to those who embrace it.

text:
Keep your heart with all diligence,
for out of it is the wellspring of life.
summary: Protect your inner self carefully because it influences everything in your life.

text:
Put away from yourself a perverse mouth.
Put corrupt lips far from you.
summary: Avoid using harmful or dishonest words in your speech.

text:
Let your eyes look straight ahead.
Fix your gaze directly before you.
summary: Stay focused on your goals and avoid distractions by keeping your attention straight ahead.

text:
Make the path of your feet level.
Let all of your ways be established.
summary: Plan your steps carefully and ensure your actions are steady and well-founded.

text:
Don’t turn to the right hand nor to the left.
Remove your foot from evil.
summary: Stay focused on the right path and avoid any actions that lead to wrongdoing.

text:
My son, pay attention to my wisdom.
Turn your ear to my understanding:
summary: Listen carefully to wise advice and understanding to guide your life.

text:
that you may maintain discretion,
that your lips may preserve knowledge.
summary: Use discretion in your speech to protect and share valuable knowledge wisely.

text:
For the lips of an adulteress drip honey.
Her mouth is smoother than oil,
summary: An adulterous person's words can be very tempting and sweet, but they are deceptive.

text:
but in the end she is as bitter as wormwood,
and as sharp as a two-edged sword.
summary: What seems appealing at first can ultimately bring bitterness and pain.

text:
Her feet go down to death.
Her steps lead straight to Sheol.
summary: Following a destructive path leads inevitably to ruin and death.

text:
She gives no thought to the way of life.
Her ways are crooked, and she doesn’t know it.
summary: Ignoring the right path leads to unknowingly harmful choices.

text:
Now therefore, my sons, listen to me.
Don’t depart from the words of my mouth.
summary: Pay close attention and adhere to wise instruction without turning away.

text:
Remove your way far from her.
Don’t come near the door of her house,
summary: Avoid situations and places that lead you into temptation or harmful behavior.

text:
lest you give your honor to others,
and your years to the cruel one;
summary: Protect your reputation and life by avoiding harmful influences and dishonorable actions.

text:
lest strangers feast on your wealth,
and your labors enrich another man’s house.
summary: Be cautious with your resources to ensure your hard work benefits you, not others who do not deserve it.

text:
You will groan at your latter end,
when your flesh and your body are consumed,
summary: Ignoring wise counsel leads to regret and suffering later in life.

text:
and say, “How I have hated instruction,
and my heart despised reproof;
summary: Regretting the rejection of correction and guidance after experiencing its consequences.

text:
neither have I obeyed the voice of my teachers,
nor turned my ear to those who instructed me!
summary: Ignoring the guidance of teachers and instructors can lead to missed opportunities for growth.

text:
I have come to the brink of utter ruin,
among the gathered assembly.”
summary: I have nearly faced complete destruction while surrounded by others.

text:
Drink water out of your own cistern,
running water out of your own well.
summary: Be faithful and find satisfaction in your own partner rather than seeking others.

text:
Should your springs overflow in the streets,
streams of water in the public squares?
summary: Don't let your private blessings or resources be wasted or exposed inappropriately in public.

text:
Let them be for yourself alone,
not for strangers with you.
summary: Keep your intimate relationships exclusive and private, not shared with outsiders.

text:
Let your spring be blessed.
Rejoice in the wife of your youth.
summary: Find joy and satisfaction in the spouse you have loved from your youth.

text:
A loving doe and a graceful deer—
let her breasts satisfy you at all times.
Be captivated always with her love.
summary: Stay deeply connected and continually cherish the love and intimacy within your committed relationship.

text:
For why should you, my son, be captivated with an adulteress?
Why embrace the bosom of another?
summary: Avoid being tempted by unfaithfulness to preserve trust and loyalty in your relationships.

text:
For the ways of man are before Yahweh’s eyes.
He examines all his paths.
summary: God observes all our actions and evaluates the choices we make in life.

text:
The evil deeds of the wicked ensnare him.
The cords of his sin hold him firmly.
summary: Engaging in wicked actions traps a person in the consequences of their own wrongdoing.

text:
He will die for lack of instruction.
In the greatness of his folly, he will go astray.
summary: Without guidance and correction, a person’s foolishness will lead them to ruin.

text:
My son, if you have become collateral for your neighbor,
if you have struck your hands in pledge for a stranger;
summary: Be cautious when guaranteeing someone else's debt, especially if you don't know them well.

text:
You are trapped by the words of your mouth.
You are ensnared with the words of your mouth.
summary: Your own words can bind you like a trap, so speak carefully to avoid trouble.

text:
Do this now, my son, and deliver yourself,
since you have come into the hand of your neighbor.
Go, humble yourself.
Press your plea with your neighbor.
summary: Act promptly and humbly to resolve conflicts and restore relationships before they worsen.

text:
Give no sleep to your eyes,
nor slumber to your eyelids.
summary: Stay alert and avoid laziness to accomplish your tasks effectively.

text:
Free yourself, like a gazelle from the hand of the hunter,
like a bird from the snare of the fowler.
summary: Take decisive action to free yourself from harmful situations before it's too late.

text:
Go to the ant, you sluggard.
Consider her ways, and be wise;
summary: Observe the ant's hardworking behavior and learn to be diligent and wise in your own efforts.

text: which having no chief, overseer, or ruler,
summary: Ants work diligently without needing a leader to supervise them.

text:
provides her bread in the summer,
and gathers her food in the harvest.
summary: Prepare and work ahead during times of plenty to ensure provision in times of need.

text:
How long will you sleep, sluggard?
When will you arise out of your sleep?
summary: Don't procrastinate or be lazy; it's time to get up and take action.

text:
A little sleep, a little slumber,
a little folding of the hands to sleep:
summary: A small amount of laziness or procrastination can lead to bigger problems if not controlled.

text:
so your poverty will come as a robber,
and your scarcity as an armed man.
summary: Neglecting responsibilities can lead to sudden and severe financial hardship.

text:
A worthless person, a man of iniquity,
is he who walks with a perverse mouth;
summary: A person who speaks deceitfully and behaves wickedly is considered worthless.

text:
who winks with his eyes, who signals with his feet,
who motions with his fingers;
summary: A person who uses secretive gestures to communicate may be untrustworthy or deceitful.

text:
in whose heart is perverseness,
who devises evil continually,
who always sows discord.
summary: A person who harbors wicked intentions and constantly stirs up conflict lacks integrity and harms relationships.

text:
Therefore his calamity will come suddenly.
He will be broken suddenly, and that without remedy.
summary: Sudden consequences often follow reckless or unwise actions, leaving no chance for recovery.

text:
There are six things which Yahweh hates;
yes, seven which are an abomination to him:
summary: There are specific behaviors that are deeply detestable to God.

text:
haughty eyes, a lying tongue,
hands that shed innocent blood;
summary: Pride, dishonesty, and harming the innocent are serious moral failings to avoid.

text:
a heart that devises wicked schemes,
feet that are swift in running to mischief,
summary: A person who plans evil and eagerly pursues wrongdoing lacks integrity and harms themselves and others.

text:
a false witness who utters lies,
and he who sows discord among brothers.
summary: Avoid lying and causing conflict among close relationships, as both harm trust and unity.

text:
My son, keep your father’s commandment,
and don’t forsake your mother’s teaching.
summary: Respect and follow the guidance and teachings of your parents for a well-grounded life.

text:
Bind them continually on your heart.
Tie them around your neck.
summary: Keep important teachings close to your heart and mind at all times.

text:
When you walk, it will lead you.
When you sleep, it will watch over you.
When you awake, it will talk with you.
summary: Let wise teachings guide and protect you throughout all your daily activities.

text:
For the commandment is a lamp,
and the law is light.
Reproofs of instruction are the way of life,
summary: Guidance and correction illuminate the path to a fulfilling and righteous life.

text:
to keep you from the immoral woman,
from the flattery of the wayward wife’s tongue.
summary: Good guidance helps you avoid temptation and harmful influences.

text:
Don’t lust after her beauty in your heart,
neither let her captivate you with her eyelids.
summary: Avoid letting physical attraction control your thoughts and lead you into temptation.

text:
For a prostitute reduces you to a piece of bread.
The adulteress hunts for your precious life.
summary: Engaging with immoral relationships can lead to personal loss and danger.

text:
Can a man scoop fire into his lap,
and his clothes not be burned?
summary: Engaging in dangerous or harmful actions will inevitably lead to negative consequences.

text:
Or can one walk on hot coals,
and his feet not be scorched?
summary: Just as walking on hot coals will inevitably burn your feet, certain actions will inevitably have consequences.

text:
So is he who goes in to his neighbor’s wife.
Whoever touches her will not be unpunished.
summary: Engaging in unfaithfulness with another's spouse leads to inevitable consequences.

text:
Men don’t despise a thief,
if he steals to satisfy himself when he is hungry:
summary: People may be more understanding of wrongdoing done out of necessity than out of greed.

text:
but if he is found, he shall restore seven times.
He shall give all the wealth of his house.
summary: If someone is caught stealing, they should make full restitution, even beyond what was taken.

text:
He who commits adultery with a woman is void of understanding.
He who does it destroys his own soul.
summary: Engaging in adultery shows a lack of wisdom and harms one's own well-being.

text:
He will get wounds and dishonor.
His reproach will not be wiped away.
summary: Ignoring consequences leads to lasting harm and disgrace that cannot be easily erased.

text:
For jealousy arouses the fury of the husband.
He won’t spare in the day of vengeance.
summary: Jealousy can provoke intense anger and lead to harsh consequences in relationships.

text:
He won’t regard any ransom,
neither will he rest content, though you give many gifts.
summary: Some people cannot be appeased or won over by gifts or bribes, no matter how much you offer.

text:
My son, keep my words.
Lay up my commandments within you.
summary: Remember and internalize wise teachings to guide your life.

text:
Keep my commandments and live!
Guard my teaching as the apple of your eye.
summary: Value and protect wise teachings closely to live a fulfilling life.

text:
Bind them on your fingers.
Write them on the tablet of your heart.
summary: Keep important teachings constantly in mind and close at hand to guide your actions.

text:
Tell wisdom, “You are my sister.”
Call understanding your relative,
summary: Value wisdom and understanding as close family, embracing them intimately in your life.

text:
that they may keep you from the strange woman,
from the foreigner who flatters with her words.
summary: Heed wise teachings to protect yourself from deceptive and flattering influences.

text:
For at the window of my house,
I looked out through my lattice.
summary: A person observes from their home through a window, setting the scene for a lesson.

text:
I saw among the simple ones.
I discerned among the youths a young man void of understanding,
summary: I noticed a young person lacking understanding and discernment.

text:
passing through the street near her corner,
he went the way to her house,
summary: A man walks near a woman's house, suggesting a moment leading to temptation or encounter.

text:
in the twilight, in the evening of the day,
in the middle of the night and in the darkness.
summary: Danger or temptation can come at any time, even in the darkest hours.

text:
Behold, there a woman met him with the attire of a prostitute,
and with crafty intent.
summary: Be cautious of people who may have deceptive intentions in relationships.

text:
She is loud and defiant.
Her feet don’t stay in her house.
summary: A person who is disruptive and restless may cause trouble by not staying within proper boundaries.

text:
Now she is in the streets, now in the squares,
and lurking at every corner.
summary: This describes someone who is constantly present in public places, implying a warning to be cautious of temptation or danger lurking openly.

text:
So she caught him, and kissed him.
With an impudent face she said to him:
summary: A bold and forward approach in seduction can lead to entrapment.

text:
“Sacrifices of peace offerings are with me.
Today I have paid my vows.
summary: Fulfilling promises and offerings reflects commitment to one's word and faith.

text:
Therefore I came out to meet you,
to diligently seek your face,
and I have found you.
summary: Seeking someone earnestly can lead to meaningful connection.

text:
I have spread my couch with carpets of tapestry,
with striped cloths of the yarn of Egypt.
summary: The speaker describes preparing a luxurious setting to entice someone, highlighting seduction and temptation.

text: I have perfumed my bed with myrrh, aloes, and cinnamon.
summary: Preparing a special setting with pleasant scents to create an inviting atmosphere in a relationship.

text:
Come, let’s take our fill of loving until the morning.
Let’s solace ourselves with loving.
summary: Encouraging indulgence in love and intimacy throughout the night.

text:
For my husband isn’t at home.
He has gone on a long journey.
summary: This verse highlights a situation involving absence in a relationship, which may lead to vulnerability or temptation.

text:
He has taken a bag of money with him.
He will come home at the full moon.”
summary: He has money and plans to return home at the full moon.

text:
With persuasive words, she led him astray.
With the flattering of her lips, she seduced him.
summary: Persuasive and flattering words can be used to mislead and entice someone into making poor choices.

text:
He followed her immediately,
as an ox goes to the slaughter,
as a fool stepping into a noose.
summary: Impulsive actions without wisdom can lead to inevitable harm, like an ox blindly heading to slaughter or a fool walking into a trap.

text:
Until an arrow strikes through his liver,
as a bird hurries to the snare,
and doesn’t know that it will cost his life.
summary: Ignoring warnings and rushing into temptation can lead to sudden and severe consequences.

text:
Now therefore, sons, listen to me.
Pay attention to the words of my mouth.
summary: Listen carefully and pay close attention to wise instruction.

text:
Don’t let your heart turn to her ways.
Don’t go astray in her paths,
summary: Stay vigilant and avoid following harmful or tempting influences that lead you away from the right path.

text:
for she has thrown down many wounded.
Yes, all her slain are a mighty army.
summary: Her actions have caused many to suffer deeply, like a powerful army of victims.

text:
Her house is the way to Sheol,
going down to the rooms of death.
summary: Falling into temptation can lead to destructive consequences, so one must exercise caution and self-control.

text:
Doesn’t wisdom cry out?
Doesn’t understanding raise her voice?
summary: Wisdom actively calls out to be heard and understood by everyone.

text:
On the top of high places by the way,
where the paths meet, she stands.
summary: Wisdom is readily available and stands at prominent crossroads, ready to guide those who seek it.

text:
Beside the gates, at the entry of the city,
at the entry doors, she cries aloud:
summary: Wisdom calls out publicly at the city's entrance, inviting all to listen.

text:
“To you men, I call!
I send my voice to the sons of mankind.
summary: Wisdom calls out to all people, inviting them to listen and learn.

text:
You simple, understand prudence.
You fools, be of an understanding heart.
summary: Those lacking insight should seek prudence and develop an understanding heart.

text:
Hear, for I will speak excellent things.
The opening of my lips is for right things.
summary: Listen carefully because I will share valuable and righteous words.

text:
For my mouth speaks truth.
Wickedness is an abomination to my lips.
summary: Always speak truth and reject wickedness in your words.

text:
All the words of my mouth are in righteousness.
There is nothing crooked or perverse in them.
summary: Speak only words that are honest and upright, free from deceit or distortion.

text:
They are all plain to him who understands,
right to those who find knowledge.
summary: Truths become clear and straightforward to those who seek and understand knowledge.

text:
Receive my instruction rather than silver;
knowledge rather than choice gold.
summary: Value learning and wisdom more than material wealth or riches.

text:
For wisdom is better than rubies.
All the things that may be desired can’t be compared to it.
summary: True wisdom is more valuable than the most precious treasures and surpasses all other desires.

text:
“I, wisdom, have made prudence my dwelling.
Find out knowledge and discretion.
summary: Wisdom is closely linked with prudence, knowledge, and good judgment, which are essential to making wise decisions.

text:
The fear of Yahweh is to hate evil.
I hate pride, arrogance, the evil way, and the perverse mouth.
summary: Respecting God means rejecting evil, pride, and arrogance in our words and actions.

text:
Counsel and sound knowledge are mine.
I have understanding and power.
summary: True wisdom combines good advice, deep understanding, and the strength to act wisely.

text:
By me kings reign,
and princes decree justice.
summary: Wise guidance empowers leaders to govern justly and make fair decisions.

text:
By me princes rule;
nobles, and all the righteous rulers of the earth.
summary: True leadership and righteous governance depend on embracing wisdom.

text:
I love those who love me.
Those who seek me diligently will find me.
summary: If you genuinely seek wisdom and understanding, you will find it and be rewarded with its benefits.

text:
With me are riches, honor,
enduring wealth, and prosperity.
summary: True wealth includes lasting riches, honor, and prosperity that come from wisdom.

text:
My fruit is better than gold, yes, than fine gold;
my yield than choice silver.
summary: True value and prosperity come from wisdom and its benefits, which surpass material riches like gold and silver.

text:
I walk in the way of righteousness,
in the middle of the paths of justice;
summary: Living a righteous life means consistently following the path of justice and fairness.

text:
that I may give wealth to those who love me.
I fill their treasuries.
summary: Loving and valuing wisdom leads to true wealth and abundance.

text:
“Yahweh possessed me in the beginning of his work,
before his deeds of old.
summary: Wisdom existed with God from the very beginning of creation.

text:
I was set up from everlasting, from the beginning,
before the earth existed.
summary: Wisdom existed from the very beginning, even before the creation of the earth.

text:
When there were no depths, I was born,
when there were no springs abounding with water.
summary: Wisdom existed before the creation of natural features like springs and depths.

text:
Before the mountains were settled in place,
before the hills, I was born;
summary: Wisdom existed before the creation of the world, highlighting its eternal nature.

text:
while as yet he had not made the earth, nor the fields,
nor the beginning of the dust of the world.
summary: This verse refers to a time before creation, highlighting the origin of the earth and fields.

text:
When he established the heavens, I was there;
when he set a circle on the surface of the deep,
summary: Wisdom was present with God during the creation of the world, highlighting its foundational role.

text:
when he established the clouds above,
when the springs of the deep became strong,
summary: This proverb reflects on the creation of natural elements like clouds and springs, highlighting the foundational role of wisdom in the world's formation.
Setting clear boundaries and order is a fundamental aspect of wise creation and governance.

text:
then I was the craftsman by his side.
I was a delight day by day,
always rejoicing before him,
summary: Wisdom is portrayed as a joyful companion in creation, highlighting its value and presence alongside God.

text:
rejoicing in his whole world.
My delight was with the sons of men.
summary: Wisdom finds joy and takes delight in humanity and the world.

text:
“Now therefore, my sons, listen to me,
for blessed are those who keep my ways.
summary: Listen carefully and follow wise guidance to receive blessings.

text:
Hear instruction, and be wise.
Don’t refuse it.
summary: Listen to guidance and accept it to grow in wisdom.

text:
Blessed is the man who hears me,
watching daily at my gates,
waiting at my door posts.
summary: Those who consistently seek and embrace wisdom are truly blessed.

text:
For whoever finds me, finds life,
and will obtain favor from Yahweh.
summary: Those who embrace wisdom gain true life and receive God's favor.

text:
But he who sins against me wrongs his own soul.
All those who hate me love death.”
summary: Choosing to live with integrity protects your well-being, while rejecting it harms your own soul and leads to destruction.

text:
Wisdom has built her house.
She has carved out her seven pillars.
summary: True wisdom establishes a strong and well-founded life, symbolized by a house with solid pillars.

text:
She has prepared her meat.
She has mixed her wine.
She has also set her table.
summary: Wisdom is being personified as preparing a feast, inviting others to partake in her offerings.

text:
She has sent out her maidens.
She cries from the highest places of the city:
summary: Wisdom publicly invites people to learn and grow, calling out from prominent places.

text:
“Whoever is simple, let him turn in here!”
As for him who is void of understanding, she says to him,
summary: Those lacking understanding are invited to seek wisdom and guidance.

text:
“Come, eat some of my bread,
Drink some of the wine which I have mixed!
summary: An invitation to share food and drink symbolizes welcoming others into fellowship and community.

text:
Leave your simple ways, and live.
Walk in the way of understanding.”
summary: Abandon ignorance and choose to live by gaining understanding and insight.

text:
He who corrects a mocker invites insult.
He who reproves a wicked man invites abuse.
summary: Correcting those who are mockers or wicked often leads to negative reactions, so choose your battles wisely.

text:
Don’t reprove a scoffer, lest he hate you.
Reprove a wise man, and he will love you.
summary: Criticism is best received by those who are wise, while scoffers may respond with hostility.

text:
Instruct a wise man, and he will be still wiser.
Teach a righteous man, and he will increase in learning.
summary: A wise person welcomes instruction and grows even wiser through learning.

text:
The fear of Yahweh is the beginning of wisdom.
The knowledge of the Holy One is understanding.
summary: Respecting and revering God is the foundation of true wisdom and understanding.

text:
For by me your days will be multiplied.
The years of your life will be increased.
summary: Embracing wisdom leads to a longer and more fulfilling life.

text:
If you are wise, you are wise for yourself.
If you mock, you alone will bear it.
summary: Your wisdom benefits only you, and if you choose to mock, you alone will suffer the consequences.

text:
The foolish woman is loud,
undisciplined, and knows nothing.
summary: A lack of self-control and knowledge leads to disruptive behavior.

text:
She sits at the door of her house,
on a seat in the high places of the city,
summary: This proverb describes a woman sitting at a prominent place, but without more context, its practical meaning is unclear.

text:
To call to those who pass by,
who go straight on their ways,
summary: This phrase alone is unclear and lacks a complete message without additional context.

text:
“Whoever is simple, let him turn in here.”
as for him who is void of understanding, she says to him,
summary: Those lacking understanding are invited to seek wisdom.

text:
“Stolen water is sweet.
Food eaten in secret is pleasant.”
summary: Things gained through wrongdoing or secrecy may seem enjoyable but are ultimately unwise.

text:
But he doesn’t know that the departed spirits are there,
that her guests are in the depths of Sheol.
summary: Some people fail to realize the serious consequences awaiting those who follow folly.
A child's wisdom brings joy to their parents, while foolishness causes them sorrow.

text:
Treasures of wickedness profit nothing,
but righteousness delivers from death.
summary: Ill-gotten gains are ultimately worthless, but living righteously brings true life and safety.

text:
Yahweh will not allow the soul of the righteous to go hungry,
but he thrusts away the desire of the wicked.
summary: God provides for the needs of the righteous, while the desires of the wicked remain unfulfilled.

text:
He becomes poor who works with a lazy hand,
but the hand of the diligent brings wealth.
summary: Laziness leads to poverty, while hard work results in prosperity.

text:
He who gathers in summer is a wise son,
but he who sleeps during the harvest is a son who causes shame.
summary: A wise person works hard and prepares during the right time, while laziness leads to shame and missed opportunities.

text:
Blessings are on the head of the righteous,
but violence covers the mouth of the wicked.
summary: Good people receive blessings and honor, while the wicked are marked by harmful words and actions.

text:
The memory of the righteous is blessed,
but the name of the wicked will rot.
summary: Good people are remembered with honor, while the reputation of the wicked fades away.

text:
The wise in heart accept commandments,
but a chattering fool will fall.
summary: A wise person listens and learns from guidance, while a foolish person who talks too much will face consequences.

text:
He who walks blamelessly walks surely,
but he who perverts his ways will be found out.
summary: Living with integrity leads to security, while deceit ultimately leads to exposure.

text:
One winking with the eye causes sorrow,
but a chattering fool will fall.
summary: Deceptive or careless speech leads to trouble, while foolish talk brings downfall.

text:
The mouth of the righteous is a spring of life,
but violence covers the mouth of the wicked.
summary: Righteous words bring life and healing, while wicked words cause harm and destruction.

text:
Hatred stirs up strife,
but love covers all wrongs.
summary: Hatred causes conflict, but love can forgive and heal all offenses.

text:
Wisdom is found on the lips of him who has discernment,
but a rod is for the back of him who is void of understanding.
summary: Those who speak with understanding share valuable wisdom, while discipline is necessary for those lacking insight.

text:
Wise men lay up knowledge,
but the mouth of the foolish is near ruin.
summary: Wise people gather and keep knowledge, while foolish talk leads to trouble.

text:
The rich man’s wealth is his strong city.
The destruction of the poor is their poverty.
summary: Wealth can provide security and protection, while poverty can lead to vulnerability and hardship.

text:
The labor of the righteous leads to life.
The increase of the wicked leads to sin.
summary: Hard work by good people brings life and blessing, while gains by the wicked lead to trouble.

text:
He is in the way of life who heeds correction,
but he who forsakes reproof leads others astray.
summary: Listening to correction guides you toward a good life, while ignoring it causes harm to yourself and others.

text:
He who hides hatred has lying lips.
He who utters a slander is a fool.
summary: Concealing hatred leads to dishonesty, and spreading slander shows foolishness.

text:
In the multitude of words there is no lack of disobedience,
but he who restrains his lips does wisely.
summary: Speaking less and controlling your words demonstrates wisdom and helps avoid trouble.

text:
The tongue of the righteous is like choice silver.
The heart of the wicked is of little worth.
summary: Words spoken by a righteous person are valuable and precious, while the intentions of the wicked lack true worth.

text:
The lips of the righteous feed many,
but the foolish die for lack of understanding.
summary: Wise and righteous words nourish and benefit many, while foolishness leads to harm and loss.

text:
Yahweh’s blessing brings wealth,
and he adds no trouble to it.
summary: True blessings from God bring prosperity without added problems.

text:
It is a fool’s pleasure to do wickedness,
but wisdom is a man of understanding’s pleasure.
summary: Fools find joy in doing wrong, but wise people take pleasure in understanding and doing what is right.

text:
What the wicked fear, will overtake them,
but the desire of the righteous will be granted.
summary: Those who do wrong are often caught by their own fears, while those who live rightly see their hopes fulfilled.

text:
When the whirlwind passes, the wicked is no more;
but the righteous stand firm forever.
summary: Those who live righteously endure life's storms, while the wicked are ultimately swept away.

text:
As vinegar to the teeth, and as smoke to the eyes,
so is the sluggard to those who send him.
summary: A lazy person is as irritating and harmful to those who rely on them as vinegar is to teeth and smoke is to eyes.

text:
The fear of Yahweh prolongs days,
but the years of the wicked shall be shortened.
summary: Respecting and revering God leads to a longer, more fulfilling life, while wickedness shortens one's days.

text:
The prospect of the righteous is joy,
but the hope of the wicked will perish.
summary: A hopeful future brings joy to the righteous, while the wicked's hopes ultimately lead to disappointment.

text:
The way of Yahweh is a stronghold to the upright,
but it is a destruction to the workers of iniquity.
summary: Following God's path provides protection for the righteous but leads to ruin for evildoers.

text:
The righteous will never be removed,
but the wicked will not dwell in the land.
summary: Those who live righteously will have lasting stability, while the wicked will ultimately lose their place.

text:
The mouth of the righteous produces wisdom,
but the perverse tongue will be cut off.
summary: A righteous person's words bring wisdom, while corrupt speech leads to ruin.

text:
The lips of the righteous know what is acceptable,
but the mouth of the wicked is perverse.
summary: Righteous people speak what is right and acceptable, while the wicked speak corruptly.

text:
A false balance is an abomination to Yahweh,
but accurate weights are his delight.
summary: Honest and fair dealings are pleasing to God, while dishonesty is detestable.

text:
When pride comes, then comes shame,
but with humility comes wisdom.
summary: Pride leads to disgrace, but embracing humility brings true wisdom.

text:
The integrity of the upright shall guide them,
but the perverseness of the treacherous shall destroy them.
summary: Being honest and upright leads to guidance and safety, while deceitfulness leads to ruin.

text:
Riches don’t profit in the day of wrath,
but righteousness delivers from death.
summary: Material wealth cannot save you in times of crisis, but living righteously offers true protection.

text:
The righteousness of the blameless will direct his way,
but the wicked shall fall by his own wickedness.
summary: Living with integrity guides a person safely, while wickedness leads to their downfall.

text:
The righteousness of the upright shall deliver them,
but the unfaithful will be trapped by evil desires.
summary: Living with integrity protects you, while giving in to unfaithfulness leads to harmful consequences.

text:
When a wicked man dies, hope perishes,
and expectation of power comes to nothing.
summary: When a wicked person dies, their hopes and expectations for power end, reminding us that wickedness leads to ultimate futility.

text:
A righteous person is delivered out of trouble,
and the wicked takes his place.
summary: Living righteously helps you avoid trouble, while wrongdoing often leads to it.

text:
With his mouth the godless man destroys his neighbor,
but the righteous will be delivered through knowledge.
summary: Harmful words from the wicked can hurt others, but the wise are saved by their understanding.

text:
When it goes well with the righteous, the city rejoices.
When the wicked perish, there is shouting.
summary: The well-being of good people brings joy to the community, while the downfall of the wicked is met with celebration.

text:
By the blessing of the upright, the city is exalted,
but it is overthrown by the mouth of the wicked.
summary: A community prospers through the positive influence of honest people, but is harmed by the destructive words of the wicked.

text:
One who despises his neighbor is void of wisdom,
but a man of understanding holds his peace.
summary: A wise person refrains from belittling others and chooses to remain silent when appropriate.

text:
One who brings gossip betrays a confidence,
but one who is of a trustworthy spirit is one who keeps a secret.
summary: A trustworthy person respects confidences, while a gossip breaks trust by revealing secrets.

text:
Where there is no wise guidance, the nation falls,
but in the multitude of counselors there is victory.
summary: A community or nation thrives with wise advice and many counselors, but without guidance, it fails.

text:
He who is collateral for a stranger will suffer for it,
but he who refuses pledges of collateral is secure.
summary: Avoid guaranteeing debts for strangers to protect yourself from financial risk.

text:
A gracious woman obtains honor,
but violent men obtain riches.
summary: A kind and gracious person earns respect, while aggressive people may gain wealth.

text:
The merciful man does good to his own soul,
but he who is cruel troubles his own flesh.
summary: Showing mercy benefits your own well-being, while cruelty harms yourself.

text:
Wicked people earn deceitful wages,
but one who sows righteousness reaps a sure reward.
summary: Dishonest gain is unreliable, but living righteously leads to lasting rewards.

text:
He who is truly righteous gets life.
He who pursues evil gets death.
summary: Living a righteous life leads to true fulfillment, while chasing evil leads to destruction.

text:
Those who are perverse in heart are an abomination to Yahweh,
but those whose ways are blameless are his delight.
summary: God values sincere and upright hearts and is displeased with those who are morally corrupt.

text:
Most certainly, the evil man will not be unpunished,
but the offspring of the righteous will be delivered.
summary: Evil actions lead to consequences, but the descendants of the righteous will find protection and deliverance.

text:
Like a gold ring in a pig’s snout,
is a beautiful woman who lacks discretion.
summary: External beauty is diminished without good judgment and discretion.

text:
The desire of the righteous is only good.
The expectation of the wicked is wrath.
summary: Good people hope for positive outcomes, while the wicked expect punishment.

text:
There is one who scatters, and increases yet more.
There is one who withholds more than is appropriate, but gains poverty.
summary: Generosity can lead to greater abundance, while excessive stinginess can result in lack.

text:
The liberal soul shall be made fat.
He who waters shall be watered also himself.
summary: Generosity leads to personal blessing and abundance in return.

text:
People curse someone who withholds grain,
but blessing will be on the head of him who sells it.
summary: Those who selfishly hoard resources may face criticism, but those who provide and sell them gain blessings.

text:
He who diligently seeks good seeks favor,
but he who searches after evil, it shall come to him.
summary: Those who earnestly pursue good will find favor, but those who look for evil will encounter it.

text:
He who trusts in his riches will fall,
but the righteous shall flourish as the green leaf.
summary: Relying on wealth alone leads to downfall, but living righteously leads to true prosperity and growth.

text:
He who troubles his own house shall inherit the wind.
The foolish shall be servant to the wise of heart.
summary: Causing strife within your own family leads to emptiness, while wisdom brings respect and leadership.

text:
The fruit of the righteous is a tree of life.
He who is wise wins souls.
summary: A righteous person's actions bring life and benefit to others, and wisdom leads to positively influencing people.

text:
Behold, the righteous shall be repaid in the earth;
how much more the wicked and the sinner!
summary: Righteous people will receive their due reward, and the consequences for the wicked will be even greater.

text:
Whoever loves correction loves knowledge,
but he who hates reproof is stupid.
summary: Embracing correction leads to gaining knowledge, while rejecting it shows foolishness.

text:
A good man shall obtain favor from Yahweh,
but he will condemn a man of wicked plans.
summary: Living with integrity brings divine favor, while wicked intentions lead to condemnation.

text:
A man shall not be established by wickedness,
but the root of the righteous shall not be moved.
summary: A person who lives by wickedness will not stand firm, but the foundation of the righteous is secure and unshakable.

text:
A worthy woman is the crown of her husband,
but a disgraceful wife is as rottenness in his bones.
summary: A good spouse brings honor and strength to their partner, while a harmful one causes deep distress.

text:
The thoughts of the righteous are just,
but the advice of the wicked is deceitful.
summary: Righteous people think and plan with fairness, while the wicked offer misleading advice.

text:
The words of the wicked are about lying in wait for blood,
but the speech of the upright rescues them.
summary: The harmful intentions behind the words of the wicked contrast with the protective and life-saving power of honest speech from the upright.

text:
The wicked are overthrown, and are no more,
but the house of the righteous shall stand.
summary: Those who live wickedly will ultimately fail, but those who live righteously will endure.

text:
A man shall be commended according to his wisdom,
but he who has a warped mind shall be despised.
summary: People are respected for their wisdom, but those with twisted thinking lose respect.

text:
Better is he who is little know, and has a servant,
than he who honors himself, and lacks bread.
summary: It's better to be modest and have your needs met than to be prideful and go without.

text:
A righteous man respects the life of his animal,
but the tender mercies of the wicked are cruel.
summary: A person of integrity shows kindness and respect even to animals, unlike the insincere compassion of the wicked.

text:
He who tills his land shall have plenty of bread,
but he who chases fantasies is void of understanding.
summary: Those who work hard and focus on practical efforts will be rewarded, while those who pursue unrealistic dreams lack good sense.

text:
The wicked desires the plunder of evil men,
but the root of the righteous flourishes.
summary: While the wicked seek to gain from wrongdoing, those who live righteously will grow and prosper steadily.

text:
An evil man is trapped by sinfulness of lips,
but the righteous shall come out of trouble.
summary: A person's harmful words can lead them into trouble, but those who live rightly will find a way out of difficulties.

text:
A man shall be satisfied with good by the fruit of his mouth.
The work of a man’s hands shall be rewarded to him.
summary: What you say and do will bring you either satisfaction or reward, so speak and act wisely.

text:
The way of a fool is right in his own eyes,
but he who is wise listens to counsel.
summary: A wise person values and learns from advice, while a fool trusts only their own judgment.

text:
A fool shows his annoyance the same day,
but one who overlooks an insult is prudent.
summary: A wise person controls their anger and doesn't react immediately to insults, showing prudence.

text:
He who is truthful testifies honestly,
but a false witness lies.
summary: Being honest and truthful is essential, while lying harms trust and justice.

text:
There is one who speaks rashly like the piercing of a sword,
but the tongue of the wise heals.
summary: Words spoken without thought can hurt deeply, but wise words bring healing and comfort.

text:
Truth’s lips will be established forever,
but a lying tongue is only momentary.
summary: Honest words have lasting value, but lies are fleeting and temporary.

text:
Deceit is in the heart of those who plot evil,
but joy comes to the promoters of peace.
summary: Those who harbor deceit and plan evil bring harm, while those who foster peace experience joy.

text:
No mischief shall happen to the righteous,
but the wicked shall be filled with evil.
summary: Those who live righteously are protected from harm, while the wicked experience the consequences of their evil actions.

text:
Lying lips are an abomination to Yahweh,
but those who do the truth are his delight.
summary: Honesty pleases God, while lying is detestable to Him.

text:
A prudent man keeps his knowledge,
but the hearts of fools proclaim foolishness.
summary: A wise person exercises discretion in sharing knowledge, while fools openly reveal their foolishness.

text:
The hands of the diligent ones shall rule,
but laziness ends in slave labor.
summary: Hard work leads to leadership and success, while laziness results in subjugation and hardship.

text:
Anxiety in a man’s heart weighs it down,
but a kind word makes it glad.
summary: Worry burdens the heart, but a kind word lifts the spirit.

text:
A righteous person is cautious in friendship,
but the way of the wicked leads them astray.
summary: A wise person chooses friends carefully, while the wicked are easily misled by their associations.

text:
The slothful man doesn’t roast his game,
but the possessions of diligent men are prized.
summary: Lazy people fail to prepare and benefit, but hardworking individuals value and enjoy their rewards.

text:
In the way of righteousness is life;
in its path there is no death.
summary: Living according to righteousness leads to true life and enduring well-being.

text:
A wise son listens to his father’s instruction,
but a scoffer doesn’t listen to rebuke.
summary: A wise person values and learns from guidance, while a mocker rejects correction.

text:
By the fruit of his lips, a man enjoys good things;
but the unfaithful crave violence.
summary: The words we speak can bring us blessings or harm, reflecting our true character.

text:
He who guards his mouth guards his soul.
One who opens wide his lips comes to ruin.
summary: Carefully controlling what you say protects your well-being, while reckless talk leads to trouble.

text:
The soul of the sluggard desires, and has nothing,
but the desire of the diligent shall be fully satisfied.
summary: Those who are lazy want things but get nothing, while those who work hard will have their desires fulfilled.

text:
A righteous man hates lies,
but a wicked man brings shame and disgrace.
summary: A person of integrity rejects falsehood, while a wicked person causes dishonor.

text:
Righteousness guards the way of integrity,
but wickedness overthrows the sinner.
summary: Living righteously protects your integrity, while wicked actions lead to downfall.

text:
There are some who pretend to be rich, yet have nothing.
There are some who pretend to be poor, yet have great wealth.
summary: Appearances can be deceiving; true wealth or poverty isn't always visible.

text:
The ransom of a man’s life is his riches,
but the poor hear no threats.
summary: Money can protect a person's life or freedom, but those without wealth are more vulnerable to danger.

text:
The light of the righteous shines brightly,
but the lamp of the wicked is snuffed out.
summary: A righteous person's life brings lasting clarity and guidance, while the wicked's influence fades away.

text:
Pride only breeds quarrels,
but with ones who take advice is wisdom.
summary: Pride leads to conflict, but those who listen to advice gain wisdom.

text:
Wealth gained dishonestly dwindles away,
but he who gathers by hand makes it grow.
summary: Ill-gotten wealth disappears quickly, but wealth earned through honest, steady work increases over time.

text:
Hope deferred makes the heart sick,
but when longing is fulfilled, it is a tree of life.
summary: Delaying hope causes emotional pain, but achieving what you desire brings deep joy and vitality.

text:
Whoever despises instruction will pay for it,
but he who respects a command will be rewarded.
summary: Respecting and valuing guidance leads to rewards, while ignoring it results in consequences.

text:
The teaching of the wise is a spring of life,
to turn from the snares of death.
summary: Learning from wise instruction brings life and helps avoid destructive pitfalls.

text:
Good understanding wins favor;
but the way of the unfaithful is hard.
summary: Having good understanding earns you goodwill, while being unfaithful leads to a difficult life.

text:
Every prudent man acts from knowledge,
but a fool exposes folly.
summary: A wise person makes decisions based on understanding, while a fool reveals their lack of sense.

text:
A wicked messenger falls into trouble,
but a trustworthy envoy gains healing.
summary: A dishonest messenger causes problems, but a reliable one brings peace and resolution.

text:
Poverty and shame come to him who refuses discipline,
but he who heeds correction shall be honored.
summary: Rejecting discipline leads to hardship and disgrace, but accepting correction brings respect and honor.

text:
Longing fulfilled is sweet to the soul,
but fools detest turning from evil.
summary: Achieving your desires brings deep satisfaction, but foolish people resist changing their harmful ways.

text:
One who walks with wise men grows wise,
but a companion of fools suffers harm.
summary: Surrounding yourself with wise people helps you become wise, while keeping company with foolish people leads to trouble.

text:
Misfortune pursues sinners,
but prosperity rewards the righteous.
summary: Those who live righteously tend to experience blessings, while wrongdoing often leads to trouble.

text:
A good man leaves an inheritance to his children’s children,
but the wealth of the sinner is stored for the righteous.
summary: A good person plans to provide for future generations, while ill-gotten wealth ultimately benefits the righteous.

text:
An abundance of food is in poor people’s fields,
but injustice sweeps it away.
summary: Even when poor people have plenty, injustice can take it all away.

text:
One who spares the rod hates his son,
but one who loves him is careful to discipline him.
summary: Loving a child means providing proper discipline to guide their growth and character.

text:
The righteous one eats to the satisfying of his soul,
but the belly of the wicked goes hungry.
summary: A person who lives rightly finds true satisfaction, while those who act wickedly experience lack and want.

text:
Every wise woman builds her house,
but the foolish one tears it down with her own hands.
summary: A wise woman strengthens and supports her family, while a foolish one causes its downfall through her actions.

text:
He who walks in his uprightness fears Yahweh,
but he who is perverse in his ways despises him.
summary: Living honestly and morally reflects reverence for God, while crooked behavior shows disregard for Him.

text:
The fool’s talk brings a rod to his back,
but the lips of the wise protect them.
summary: Foolish words lead to trouble, but wise speech offers protection.

text:
Where no oxen are, the crib is clean,
but much increase is by the strength of the ox.
summary: Without effort or resources, things may be simple, but progress and growth come through hard work and support.

text:
A truthful witness will not lie,
but a false witness pours out lies.
summary: Honest people speak the truth, while dishonest people spread lies.

text:
A scoffer seeks wisdom, and doesn’t find it,
but knowledge comes easily to a discerning person.
summary: Those who mock or scoff at wisdom fail to gain it, while those who are discerning acquire knowledge with ease.

text:
Stay away from a foolish man,
for you won’t find knowledge on his lips.
summary: Avoid associating with foolish people because they do not offer valuable knowledge or insight.

text:
The wisdom of the prudent is to think about his way,
but the folly of fools is deceit.
summary: Wise people carefully consider their actions, while fools are characterized by deceitfulness.

text:
Fools mock at making atonement for sins,
but among the upright there is good will.
summary: Fools disregard the importance of seeking forgiveness, but those with integrity foster goodwill.

text:
The heart knows its own bitterness and joy;
he will not share these with a stranger.
summary: People deeply understand their own feelings and often keep them private from others.

text:
The house of the wicked will be overthrown,
but the tent of the upright will flourish.
summary: Those who live wickedly will face ruin, but those who live righteously will prosper.

text:
There is a way which seems right to a man,
but in the end it leads to death.
summary: What seems right to us may ultimately lead to harm, so seek true wisdom beyond your own judgment.

text:
Even in laughter the heart may be sorrowful,
and mirth may end in heaviness.
summary: Sometimes, outward joy can hide inner sadness, reminding us to look beyond appearances.

text:
The unfaithful will be repaid for his own ways;
likewise a good man will be rewarded for his ways.
summary: People will face consequences or rewards based on their own actions and character.

text:
A simple man believes everything,
but the prudent man carefully considers his ways.
summary: A naive person trusts everything they hear, but a wise person thinks carefully before acting.

text:
A wise man fears and shuns evil,
but the fool is hot headed and reckless.
summary: A truly wise person avoids evil out of caution, while a fool acts impulsively and carelessly.

text:
He who is quick to become angry will commit folly,
and a crafty man is hated.
summary: Quick temper leads to foolish mistakes, and deceitful behavior causes others to dislike you.

text:
The simple inherit folly,
but the prudent are crowned with knowledge.
summary: Those who are naive end up with foolishness, but those who are careful and wise gain knowledge and honor.

text:
The evil bow down before the good,
and the wicked at the gates of the righteous.
summary: Those who do wrong ultimately show respect or submission to those who live rightly.

text:
The poor person is shunned even by his own neighbor,
but the rich person has many friends.
summary: People often distance themselves from those who are poor but seek the company of the wealthy.

text:
He who despises his neighbor sins,
but blessed is he who has pity on the poor.
summary: Showing kindness and compassion to others, especially those in need, is a moral duty that brings blessings.

text:
Don’t they go astray who plot evil?
But love and faithfulness belong to those who plan good.
summary: Those who scheme evil will fail, but those who plan good will be rewarded with love and faithfulness.

text:
In all hard work there is profit,
but the talk of the lips leads only to poverty.
summary: Hard work leads to gain, but empty talk results in loss.

text:
The crown of the wise is their riches,
but the folly of fools crowns them with folly.
summary: True wealth comes from wisdom, while foolishness only leads to more foolishness.

text:
A truthful witness saves souls,
but a false witness is deceitful.
summary: Honest testimony protects and benefits others, while dishonesty causes harm and betrayal.

text:
In the fear of Yahweh is a secure fortress,
and he will be a refuge for his children.
summary: Respecting and revering God provides a strong foundation of security and protection for those who trust in Him.

text:
The fear of Yahweh is a fountain of life,
turning people from the snares of death.
summary: Respecting and revering God brings true life and protects from destructive paths.

text:
In the multitude of people is the king’s glory,
but in the lack of people is the destruction of the prince.
summary: A leader's honor is reflected by the number of their followers, while a lack of support can lead to their downfall.

text:
He who is slow to anger has great understanding,
but he who has a quick temper displays folly.
summary: Being patient and slow to anger shows true wisdom, while quick temper reveals foolishness.

text:
The life of the body is a heart at peace,
but envy rots the bones.
summary: A peaceful heart promotes health and well-being, while envy harms both mind and body.

text:
He who oppresses the poor shows contempt for his Maker,
but he who is kind to the needy honors him.
summary: Treating the poor with kindness honors God, while oppressing them shows disrespect to Him.

text:
The wicked is brought down in his calamity,
but in death, the righteous has a refuge.
summary: While the wicked suffer in adversity, the righteous find peace even in death.

text:
Wisdom rests in the heart of one who has understanding,
and is even made known in the inward part of fools.
summary: True wisdom dwells within those who understand, and even fools can sense it deep inside.

text:
Righteousness exalts a nation,
but sin is a disgrace to any people.
summary: A nation's honor and success depend on its commitment to righteousness, while sin brings shame to its people.

text:
The king’s favor is toward a servant who deals wisely,
but his wrath is toward one who causes shame.
summary: Those who act wisely gain favor and respect, while those who bring shame face disapproval and consequences.

text:
A gentle answer turns away wrath,
but a harsh word stirs up anger.
summary: Responding calmly can defuse anger, while harsh words often escalate conflict.

text:
The tongue of the wise commends knowledge,
but the mouth of fools gush out folly.
summary: Wise people speak to share knowledge, while fools speak carelessly and foolishly.

text:
Yahweh’s eyes are everywhere,
keeping watch on the evil and the good.
summary: God is always watching, so both good and bad actions are seen and accounted for.

text:
A gentle tongue is a tree of life,
but deceit in it crushes the spirit.
summary: Kind and gentle words bring life and healing, while deceitful speech harms and discourages others.

text:
A fool despises his father’s correction,
but he who heeds reproof shows prudence.
summary: Ignoring correction is foolish, but accepting it shows wisdom and good judgment.

text:
In the house of the righteous is much treasure,
but the income of the wicked brings trouble.
summary: A righteous person's home is enriched with true treasure, while the earnings of the wicked lead to problems.

text:
The lips of the wise spread knowledge;
not so with the heart of fools.
summary: Wise people share valuable knowledge through their words, but fools keep their understanding to themselves.

text:
The sacrifice made by the wicked is an abomination to Yahweh,
but the prayer of the upright is his delight.
summary: God values sincere prayers from righteous people more than offerings from the wicked.

text:
The way of the wicked is an abomination to Yahweh,
but he loves him who follows after righteousness.
summary: Living a righteous life pleases God, while wickedness is detestable to Him.

text:
There is stern discipline for one who forsakes the way:
whoever hates reproof shall die.
summary: Rejecting correction leads to serious consequences, so accepting discipline is essential for a good life.

text:
Sheol and Abaddon are before Yahweh—
how much more then the hearts of the children of men!
summary: God understands the deepest parts of our hearts even more than the realms of death and destruction.

text:
A scoffer doesn’t love to be reproved;
he will not go to the wise.
summary: People who mock others resist correction and avoid seeking wise counsel.

text:
A glad heart makes a cheerful face;
but an aching heart breaks the spirit.
summary: A joyful heart brings a happy appearance, but sadness can deeply affect one's spirit.

text:
The heart of one who has understanding seeks knowledge,
but the mouths of fools feed on folly.
summary: A wise person actively pursues knowledge, while a fool indulges in foolish talk.

text:
All the days of the afflicted are wretched,
but one who has a cheerful heart enjoys a continual feast.
summary: A positive attitude can transform difficult circumstances into a source of ongoing joy.

text:
Better is little, with the fear of Yahweh,
than great treasure with trouble.
summary: It's better to have modest possessions with reverence for God than great wealth accompanied by stress and problems.

text:
Better is a dinner of herbs, where love is,
than a fattened calf with hatred.
summary: A simple meal shared with love is better than a lavish feast accompanied by hatred.

text:
A wrathful man stirs up contention,
but one who is slow to anger appeases strife.
summary: Controlling your anger helps prevent conflicts and promotes peace.

text:
The way of the sluggard is like a thorn patch,
but the path of the upright is a highway.
summary: Laziness leads to difficulties and obstacles, while living rightly makes life smoother and more straightforward.

text:
A wise son makes a father glad,
but a foolish man despises his mother.
summary: A wise child brings joy to their parents, while a foolish person shows disrespect to their mother.

text:
Folly is joy to one who is void of wisdom,
but a man of understanding keeps his way straight.
summary: A person lacking wisdom finds pleasure in foolishness, while a wise person stays on the right path.

text:
Where there is no counsel, plans fail;
but in a multitude of counselors they are established.
summary: Plans are more likely to succeed when you seek advice from many trusted advisors.

text:
Joy comes to a man with the reply of his mouth.
How good is a word at the right time!
summary: Timely and thoughtful words bring joy and encouragement to others.

text:
The path of life leads upward for the wise,
to keep him from going downward to Sheol.
summary: Wise choices guide a person toward a fulfilling life and away from destruction.

text:
Yahweh will uproot the house of the proud,
but he will keep the widow’s borders intact.
summary: Pride leads to downfall, but God protects the vulnerable and humble.

text:
Yahweh detests the thoughts of the wicked,
but the thoughts of the pure are pleasing.
summary: God rejects evil intentions but values pure and sincere thoughts.

text:
He who is greedy for gain troubles his own house,
but he who hates bribes will live.
summary: Being greedy causes problems at home, but rejecting bribes leads to a stable life.

text:
