The heart of the righteous weighs answers,
but the mouth of the wicked gushes out evil.
summary: A wise person carefully considers their responses, while a wicked person speaks harmful words impulsively.

text:
Yahweh is far from the wicked,
but he hears the prayer of the righteous.
summary: God distances Himself from the wicked but listens attentively to the prayers of those who live righteously.

text:
The light of the eyes rejoices the heart.
Good news gives health to the bones.
summary: Seeing something joyful or receiving good news uplifts your spirit and strengthens your well-being.

text:
The ear that listens to reproof lives,
and will be at home among the wise.
summary: Being open to correction helps you grow and be accepted among wise people.

text:
He who refuses correction despises his own soul,
but he who listens to reproof gets understanding.
summary: Accepting correction leads to greater understanding, while rejecting it harms your well-being.

text:
The fear of Yahweh teaches wisdom.
Before honor is humility.
summary: Respecting God leads to wisdom, and true honor comes through humility.

text:
Better is a dry morsel with quietness,
than a house full of feasting with strife.
summary: It's better to have little and peace than abundance accompanied by conflict.

text:
A servant who deals wisely will rule over a son who causes shame,
and shall have a part in the inheritance among the brothers.
summary: A wise and responsible servant can gain authority and share in family blessings, even over an irresponsible heir.

text:
The refining pot is for silver, and the furnace for gold,
but Yahweh tests the hearts.
summary: Just as fire tests and purifies precious metals, God examines and refines our inner character.

text:
An evildoer heeds wicked lips.
A liar gives ear to a mischievous tongue.
summary: People with corrupt intentions tend to listen to and be influenced by dishonest and harmful speech.

text:
Whoever mocks the poor reproaches his Maker.
He who is glad at calamity shall not be unpunished.
summary: Disrespecting the poor is equivalent to insulting God, and rejoicing in others' misfortune will bring consequences.

text:
Children’s children are the crown of old men;
the glory of children are their parents.
summary: Grandchildren bring honor to grandparents, and parents are a source of pride for their children.

text:
Arrogant speech isn’t fitting for a fool,
much less do lying lips fit a prince.
summary: Both fools and leaders should avoid arrogant and dishonest speech, as it is inappropriate and damaging.

text:
A bribe is a precious stone in the eyes of him who gives it;
wherever he turns, he prospers.
summary: A bribe may seem valuable to the giver because it appears to bring success wherever they go.

text:
He who covers an offense promotes love;
but he who repeats a matter separates best friends.
summary: Forgiving and not dwelling on others' mistakes fosters love, while constantly bringing them up damages friendships.

text:
A rebuke enters deeper into one who has understanding
than a hundred lashes into a fool.
summary: Constructive criticism affects a wise person more profoundly than harsh punishment affects a fool.

text:
An evil man seeks only rebellion;
therefore a cruel messenger shall be sent against him.
summary: A person who pursues wrongdoing invites consequences and opposition.

text:
Let a bear robbed of her cubs meet a man,
rather than a fool in his folly.
summary: It's safer to face a dangerous animal than to deal with a foolish person acting recklessly.

text:
Whoever rewards evil for good,
evil shall not depart from his house.
summary: If you repay kindness with harm, trouble will stay in your life.

text:
The beginning of strife is like breaching a dam,
therefore stop contention before quarreling breaks out.
summary: Address conflicts early to prevent bigger disputes from arising.

text:
He who justifies the wicked, and he who condemns the righteous,
both of them alike are an abomination to Yahweh.
summary: Defending the guilty or condemning the innocent is detestable to God and undermines true justice.

text:
Why is there money in the hand of a fool to buy wisdom,
since he has no understanding?
summary: Having money doesn't guarantee understanding or the ability to gain true wisdom.

text:
A friend loves at all times;
and a brother is born for adversity.
summary: True friends show love consistently, and family is especially there to support you during tough times.

text:
A man void of understanding strikes hands,
and becomes collateral in the presence of his neighbor.
summary: A person lacking good judgment quickly makes risky commitments that can lead to serious consequences.

text:
He who loves disobedience loves strife.
One who builds a high gate seeks destruction.
summary: Those who enjoy rebellion invite conflict, and those who isolate themselves invite trouble.

text:
One who has a perverse heart doesn’t find prosperity,
and one who has a deceitful tongue falls into trouble.
summary: A person with a twisted heart and dishonest speech will face difficulties and fail to prosper.

text:
He who becomes the father of a fool grieves.
The father of a fool has no joy.
summary: Having a child who lacks wisdom brings sorrow and disappointment to a parent.

text:
A cheerful heart makes good medicine,
but a crushed spirit dries up the bones.
summary: Maintaining a positive and joyful attitude promotes health and well-being, while despair harms both mind and body.

text:
A wicked man receives a bribe in secret,
to pervert the ways of justice.
summary: Accepting bribes corrupts justice and undermines fairness.

text:
Wisdom is before the face of one who has understanding,
but the eyes of a fool wander to the ends of the earth.
summary: A person with understanding focuses on wisdom, while a fool is easily distracted and looks everywhere without gaining insight.

text:
A foolish son brings grief to his father,
and bitterness to her who bore him.
summary: A child's foolish behavior causes deep sorrow to their parents.

text:
Also to punish the righteous is not good,
nor to flog officials for their integrity.
summary: It's wrong to punish or criticize those who act with righteousness and integrity, especially leaders.

text:
He who spares his words has knowledge.
He who is even tempered is a man of understanding.
summary: A person who speaks thoughtfully and remains calm shows true understanding and knowledge.

text:
Even a fool, when he keeps silent, is counted wise.
When he shuts his lips, he is thought to be discerning.
summary: Sometimes staying silent can make a person appear wiser than they really are.

text:
An unfriendly man pursues selfishness,
and defies all sound judgment.
summary: A person who isolates themselves to chase selfish goals rejects wise counsel and sound judgment.

text:
A fool has no delight in understanding,
but only in revealing his own opinion.
summary: A foolish person prefers to express their own views rather than seek to understand others.

text:
When wickedness comes, contempt also comes,
and with shame comes disgrace.
summary: When evil actions occur, they bring contempt and shame along with them.

text:
The words of a man’s mouth are like deep waters.
The fountain of wisdom is like a flowing brook.
summary: A person's words can hold deep meaning and wisdom flows continuously like a refreshing stream.

text:
To be partial to the faces of the wicked is not good,
nor to deprive the innocent of justice.
summary: Showing favoritism to the wicked or denying justice to the innocent is morally wrong and undermines fairness.

text:
A fool’s lips come into strife,
and his mouth invites beatings.
summary: Foolish talk often leads to conflict and trouble.

text:
A fool’s mouth is his destruction,
and his lips are a snare to his soul.
summary: Careless words can lead to personal ruin and trap you in trouble.

text:
The words of a gossip are like dainty morsels:
they go down into a person’s innermost parts.
summary: Gossip can be tempting and appealing, but it deeply affects and influences people internally.

text:
One who is slack in his work
is brother to him who is a master of destruction.
summary: Being lazy in your work is as harmful as being destructive.

text:
Yahweh’s name is a strong tower:
the righteous run to him, and are safe.
summary: Trusting in God's protection provides safety and security for those who live rightly.

text:
The rich man’s wealth is his strong city,
like an unscalable wall in his own imagination.
summary: A wealthy person's riches can feel like a secure fortress, giving them a sense of protection and confidence.

text:
Before destruction the heart of man is proud,
but before honor is humility.
summary: Pride leads to downfall, but humility paves the way to honor.

text:
He who gives answer before he hears,
that is folly and shame to him.
summary: Answering before fully listening shows foolishness and leads to embarrassment.

text:
A man’s spirit will sustain him in sickness,
but a crushed spirit, who can bear?
summary: A strong and hopeful spirit can help a person endure illness, but a broken spirit is hard to withstand.

text:
The heart of the discerning gets knowledge.
The ear of the wise seeks knowledge.
summary: A wise person actively seeks and values knowledge to grow in understanding.

text:
A man’s gift makes room for him,
and brings him before great men.
summary: A thoughtful gift can open doors and create opportunities to connect with influential people.

text:
He who pleads his cause first seems right;
until another comes and questions him.
summary: The first person to present their case may seem convincing, but true understanding comes when others offer their perspective.

text:
The lot settles disputes,
and keeps strong ones apart.
summary: Sometimes leaving decisions to chance can resolve conflicts and prevent fights among strong-willed people.

text:
A brother offended is more difficult than a fortified city;
and disputes are like the bars of a fortress.
summary: Reconciliation with a hurt close friend or family member is often harder than overcoming a strong fortress.

text:
A man’s stomach is filled with the fruit of his mouth.
With the harvest of his lips he is satisfied.
summary: What you say can bring you satisfaction and results, just like a harvest from your words.

text:
Death and life are in the power of the tongue;
those who love it will eat its fruit.
summary: The words you choose have the power to bring harm or healing, so speak wisely and thoughtfully.

text:
Whoever finds a wife finds a good thing,
and obtains favor of Yahweh.
summary: Finding a good spouse is a valuable blessing and brings favor from God.

text:
The poor plead for mercy,
but the rich answer harshly.
summary: Those with less often seek kindness, while those with more may respond with severity.

text:
A man of many companions may be ruined,
but there is a friend who sticks closer than a brother.
summary: Having many acquaintances doesn't guarantee support, but a true friend is more loyal than family.

text:
Better is the poor who walks in his integrity
than he who is perverse in his lips and is a fool.
summary: It's better to be poor and honest than to be deceitful and foolish.

text:
It isn’t good to have zeal without knowledge;
nor being hasty with one’s feet and missing the way.
summary: Enthusiasm without understanding can lead to mistakes, so it's important to act thoughtfully rather than impulsively.

text:
The foolishness of man subverts his way;
his heart rages against Yahweh.
summary: A person's own foolish choices can ruin their path, especially when they resist guidance from God.

text:
Wealth adds many friends,
but the poor is separated from his friend.
summary: Having wealth can attract many friends, while poverty may cause even close friends to distance themselves.

text:
A false witness shall not be unpunished.
He who pours out lies shall not go free.
summary: Those who lie or give false testimony will face consequences and cannot escape punishment.

text:
Many will entreat the favor of a ruler,
and everyone is a friend to a man who gives gifts.
summary: People often seek the favor of those in power, and generosity attracts many friends.

text:
All the relatives of the poor shun him:
how much more do his friends avoid him!
He pursues them with pleas, but they are gone.
summary: People often distance themselves from those who are poor, even close relatives and friends, leaving the poor isolated despite their efforts to seek help.

text:
He who gets wisdom loves his own soul.
He who keeps understanding shall find good.
summary: Valuing and pursuing wisdom benefits your well-being and leads to positive outcomes.

text:
A false witness shall not be unpunished.
He who utters lies shall perish.
summary: Those who lie or give false testimony will face consequences and cannot escape punishment.

text:
Delicate living is not appropriate for a fool,
much less for a servant to have rule over princes.
summary: A person lacking wisdom should not expect to live comfortably or hold positions of great authority.

text:
The discretion of a man makes him slow to anger.
It is his glory to overlook an offense.
summary: Being patient and forgiving others is a sign of true wisdom and honor.

text:
The king’s wrath is like the roaring of a lion,
but his favor is like dew on the grass.
summary: A leader's anger can be fierce and destructive, but their favor brings refreshing blessings.

text:
A foolish son is the calamity of his father.
A wife’s quarrels are a continual dripping.
summary: A foolish child brings trouble to their parents, and constant arguing from a spouse wears down the household.

text:
House and riches are an inheritance from fathers,
but a prudent wife is from Yahweh.
summary: Material wealth can be inherited, but a wise and understanding spouse is a true blessing from God.

text:
Slothfulness casts into a deep sleep.
The idle soul shall suffer hunger.
summary: Laziness leads to poverty and unmet needs, so staying active and hardworking is essential for survival.

text:
He who keeps the commandment keeps his soul,
but he who is contemptuous in his ways shall die.
summary: Obeying moral commands preserves your well-being, while disregarding them leads to harm.

text:
He who has pity on the poor lends to Yahweh;
he will reward him.
summary: Showing kindness to the poor is like lending to God, who will repay your generosity.

text:
Discipline your son, for there is hope;
don’t be a willing party to his death.
summary: Correcting your child is an act of love that offers hope for their future and prevents harm.

text:
A hot-tempered man must pay the penalty,
for if you rescue him, you must do it again.
summary: People with quick tempers often face repeated consequences, so it's wise to address the root cause rather than repeatedly rescuing them.

text:
Listen to counsel and receive instruction,
that you may be wise in your latter end.
summary: Be open to advice and learning so you can grow wiser over time and in the future.

text:
There are many plans in a man’s heart,
but Yahweh’s counsel will prevail.
summary: People may have many ideas, but ultimately, God's guidance determines the outcome.

text:
That which makes a man to be desired is his kindness.
A poor man is better than a liar.
summary: Being kind makes a person truly admirable, and honesty is more valuable than wealth.

text:
The fear of Yahweh leads to life, then contentment;
he rests and will not be touched by trouble.
summary: Respecting and revering God leads to a fulfilling and peaceful life, free from unnecessary troubles.

text:
The sluggard buries his hand in the dish;
he will not so much as bring it to his mouth again.
summary: A lazy person starts a task but lacks the effort to complete it or benefit from it.

text:
Flog a scoffer, and the simple will learn prudence;
rebuke one who has understanding, and he will gain knowledge.
summary: Correcting a mocker teaches the naive prudence, and rebuking the wise leads to greater understanding.

text:
He who robs his father and drives away his mother,
is a son who causes shame and brings reproach.
summary: Disrespecting and harming one's parents brings shame and dishonor to oneself.

text:
If you stop listening to instruction, my son,
you will stray from the words of knowledge.
summary: Ignoring guidance leads you away from gaining true understanding.

text:
A corrupt witness mocks justice,
and the mouth of the wicked gulps down iniquity.
summary: Dishonest witnesses undermine justice, and the wicked eagerly embrace wrongdoing.

text:
Penalties are prepared for scoffers,
and beatings for the backs of fools.
summary: Those who mock correction will face consequences and punishment.

text:
Wine is a mocker, and beer is a brawler.
Whoever is led astray by them is not wise.
summary: Excessive drinking leads to poor judgment and trouble, so avoiding it shows wisdom and self-control.

text:
The terror of a king is like the roaring of a lion.
He who provokes him to anger forfeits his own life.
summary: Provoking a powerful leader's anger can lead to severe consequences, much like facing a roaring lion.

text:
It is an honor for a man to keep aloof from strife;
but every fool will be quarreling.
summary: It is wise and honorable to avoid unnecessary conflicts, while foolish people provoke quarrels.

text:
The sluggard will not plow by reason of the winter;
therefore he shall beg in harvest, and have nothing.
summary: Those who avoid hard work now will face scarcity later when they need the fruits of their labor.

text:
Counsel in the heart of man is like deep water;
but a man of understanding will draw it out.
summary: Insight and advice are often hidden within a person, but a wise individual knows how to uncover and understand them.

text:
Many men claim to be men of unfailing love,
but who can find a faithful man?
summary: Many people profess loyalty, but truly faithful individuals are rare and valuable.

text:
A righteous man walks in integrity.
Blessed are his children after him.
summary: A person who lives with integrity sets a positive example that benefits their descendants.

text:
A king who sits on the throne of judgment
scatters away all evil with his eyes.
summary: A just ruler can eliminate wrongdoing simply by exercising fair and discerning judgment.

text:
Who can say, “I have made my heart pure.
I am clean and without sin?”
summary: No one can claim to be completely pure or without fault; recognizing our imperfections is essential.

text:
Differing weights and differing measures,
both of them alike are an abomination to Yahweh.
summary: Using dishonest weights and measures is morally wrong and condemned by God.

text:
Even a child makes himself known by his doings,
whether his work is pure, and whether it is right.
summary: A person's true character is revealed through their actions, even from a young age.

text:
The hearing ear, and the seeing eye,
Yahweh has made even both of them.
summary: God has given us the abilities to hear and see, which are essential for gaining understanding and wisdom.

text:
Don’t love sleep, lest you come to poverty.
Open your eyes, and you shall be satisfied with bread.
summary: Avoid laziness and oversleeping, because hard work leads to provision and satisfaction.

text:
“It’s no good, it’s no good,” says the buyer;
but when he is gone his way, then he boasts.
summary: People often downplay the value of something when bargaining but praise it once they have it.

text:
There is gold and abundance of rubies;
but the lips of knowledge are a rare jewel.
summary: Valuable treasures like gold and rubies are common, but wise and knowledgeable speech is truly rare and precious.

text:
Take the garment of one who puts up collateral for a stranger;
and hold him in pledge for a wayward woman.
summary: Be cautious when guaranteeing debts for others, especially those who may be unreliable or lead you into trouble.

text:
Fraudulent food is sweet to a man,
but afterwards his mouth is filled with gravel.
summary: Dishonest gains may seem appealing at first, but they ultimately lead to suffering and regret.

text:
Plans are established by advice;
by wise guidance you wage war!
summary: Successful plans are made through seeking and following wise counsel.

text:
He who goes about as a tale-bearer reveals secrets;
therefore don’t keep company with him who opens wide his lips.
summary: Avoid associating with gossipers because they reveal secrets and can harm trust.

text:
Whoever curses his father or his mother,
his lamp shall be put out in blackness of darkness.
summary: Disrespecting or cursing one's parents leads to severe consequences and loss of guidance in life.

text:
An inheritance quickly gained at the beginning,
won’t be blessed in the end.
summary: Wealth gained too quickly often doesn't bring lasting blessings or stability.

text:
Don’t say, “I will pay back evil.”
Wait for Yahweh, and he will save you.
summary: Avoid seeking personal revenge; trust that justice will be served in due time.

text:
Yahweh detests differing weights,
and dishonest scales are not pleasing.
summary: God hates dishonesty, especially in business dealings like using false weights and measures.

text:
A man’s steps are from Yahweh;
how then can man understand his way?
summary: Our paths are guided by God, so we cannot fully comprehend our own direction without His guidance.

text:
It is a snare to a man to make a rash dedication,
then later to consider his vows.
summary: Making quick promises without thinking can trap you later when you have to fulfill them.

text:
A wise king winnows out the wicked,
and drives the threshing wheel over them.
summary: A wise leader removes evil influences decisively to maintain justice and order.

text:
The spirit of man is Yahweh’s lamp,
searching all his innermost parts.
summary: A person's inner spirit is like a light from God that reveals their true thoughts and feelings.

text:
Love and faithfulness keep the king safe.
His throne is sustained by love.
summary: Loyalty and faithfulness protect leaders and uphold their authority.

text:
The glory of young men is their strength.
The splendor of old men is their gray hair.
summary: Youth is valued for physical strength, while old age is honored for the wisdom symbolized by gray hair.

text:
Wounding blows cleanse away evil,
and beatings purge the innermost parts.
summary: Corrective discipline can remove harmful behaviors and purify one's character.

text:
The king’s heart is in Yahweh’s hand like the watercourses.
He turns it wherever he desires.
summary: Leaders' decisions are ultimately influenced by a higher power guiding their direction.

text:
Every way of a man is right in his own eyes,
but Yahweh weighs the hearts.
summary: People often believe their actions are justified, but true judgment comes from understanding their inner motives.

text:
To do righteousness and justice
is more acceptable to Yahweh than sacrifice.
summary: Acting justly and doing what is right pleases God more than offering sacrifices.

text:
A high look, and a proud heart,
the lamp of the wicked, is sin.
summary: Pride and arrogance are like a guiding light for the wicked, leading to sin.

text:
The plans of the diligent surely lead to profit;
and everyone who is hasty surely rushes to poverty.
summary: Careful and persistent planning leads to success, while rushing leads to failure.

text:
Getting treasures by a lying tongue
is a fleeting vapor for those who seek death.
summary: Gaining wealth through lies is ultimately empty and self-destructive.

text:
The violence of the wicked will drive them away,
because they refuse to do what is right.
summary: Those who act wickedly and refuse to do what is right will ultimately face consequences that push them away from others.

text:
The way of the guilty is devious,
but the conduct of the innocent is upright.
summary: Those who do wrong often take crooked paths, but those who are innocent live honestly and straightforwardly.

text:
It is better to dwell in the corner of the housetop,
than to share a house with a contentious woman.
summary: It's preferable to live alone in a small space than to live with someone who causes constant conflict.

text:
The soul of the wicked desires evil;
his neighbor finds no mercy in his eyes.
summary: A person with wicked intentions shows no kindness or mercy to others.

text:
When the mocker is punished, the simple gains wisdom.
When the wise is instructed, he receives knowledge.
summary: Correcting wrongdoers teaches the naive wisdom, while the wise grow wiser through instruction.

text:
The Righteous One considers the house of the wicked,
and brings the wicked to ruin.
summary: A just person observes the actions of the wicked, and ultimately, the wicked face consequences for their wrongdoing.

text:
Whoever stops his ears at the cry of the poor,
he will also cry out, but shall not be heard.
summary: Ignoring the needs of the poor will lead to a time when your own cries for help go unanswered.

text:
A gift in secret pacifies anger;
and a bribe in the cloak, strong wrath.
summary: Secretly giving a gift can calm anger, but a hidden bribe can provoke intense wrath.

text:
It is joy to the righteous to do justice;
but it is a destruction to the workers of iniquity.
summary: Righteous people find happiness in doing what is just, while evildoers bring ruin upon themselves.

text:
The man who wanders out of the way of understanding
shall rest in the assembly of the departed spirits.
summary: Those who stray from understanding risk serious consequences, even death.

text:
He who loves pleasure shall be a poor man.
He who loves wine and oil shall not be rich.
summary: Prioritizing indulgence in pleasure and luxury can lead to financial hardship.

text:
The wicked is a ransom for the righteous;
the treacherous for the upright.
summary: Sometimes, the consequences faced by the wicked can serve as a form of protection or benefit for the righteous.

text:
It is better to dwell in a desert land,
than with a contentious and fretful woman.
summary: It's preferable to live in solitude than to endure constant conflict in close relationships.

text:
There is precious treasure and oil in the dwelling of the wise;
but a foolish man swallows it up.
summary: Wise people save and manage their resources carefully, while foolish people waste them recklessly.

text:
He who follows after righteousness and kindness
finds life, righteousness, and honor.
summary: Pursuing righteousness and kindness leads to a fulfilling and honorable life.

text:
A wise man scales the city of the mighty,
and brings down the strength of its confidence.
summary: A wise person can overcome great challenges by using insight and strategy rather than brute force.

text:
Whoever guards his mouth and his tongue
keeps his soul from troubles.
summary: Carefully controlling what you say protects you from many problems.

text:
The proud and haughty man, “scoffer” is his name;
he works in the arrogance of pride.
summary: Prideful and arrogant behavior leads to a scornful attitude that damages one's character.

text:
The desire of the sluggard kills him,
for his hands refuse to labor.
summary: Laziness and unfulfilled desires can lead to ruin because success requires effort and hard work.

text:
There are those who covet greedily all day long;
but the righteous give and don’t withhold.
summary: While greedy people always want more, righteous individuals are generous and willing to share.

text:
The sacrifice of the wicked is an abomination;
how much more, when he brings it with a wicked mind!
summary: Offering gifts or sacrifices with a corrupt heart is detestable, highlighting the importance of sincere intentions.

text:
A false witness will perish,
and a man who listens speaks to eternity.
summary: False testimony leads to ruin, but those who heed truth speak with lasting impact.

text:
A wicked man hardens his face;
but as for the upright, he establishes his ways.
summary: A dishonest person is stubborn and unyielding, but a righteous person builds a stable and honorable life.

text:
There is no wisdom nor understanding
nor counsel against Yahweh.
summary: No human wisdom, understanding, or advice can prevail against God's will.

text:
The horse is prepared for the day of battle;
but victory is with Yahweh.
summary: Preparation is important, but ultimate success depends on divine favor.

text:
A good name is more desirable than great riches,
and loving favor is better than silver and gold.
summary: Having a good reputation and genuine favor is more valuable than material wealth.

text:
The rich and the poor have this in common:
Yahweh is the maker of them all.
summary: Regardless of wealth, all people share the same Creator and equal value in His eyes.

text:
A prudent man sees danger, and hides himself;
but the simple pass on, and suffer for it.
summary: A wise person anticipates danger and takes precautions, while a naive person ignores risks and faces consequences.

text:
The result of humility and the fear of Yahweh
is wealth, honor, and life.
summary: Humility and reverence for God lead to true wealth, respect, and a fulfilling life.

text:
Thorns and snares are in the path of the wicked:
whoever guards his soul stays from them.
summary: Being cautious and protecting yourself helps you avoid the dangers that come from wickedness.

text:
Train up a child in the way he should go,
and when he is old he will not depart from it.
summary: Teaching children good values early helps them maintain those values throughout their lives.

text:
The rich rule over the poor.
The borrower is servant to the lender.
summary: Debt creates a power imbalance where the borrower becomes subject to the lender.

text:
He who sows wickedness reaps trouble,
and the rod of his fury will be destroyed.
summary: Those who do evil will face consequences and their anger will ultimately be restrained.

text:
He who has a generous eye will be blessed;
for he shares his food with the poor.
summary: Generosity towards those in need brings blessings and favor.

text:
Drive out the mocker, and strife will go out;
yes, quarrels and insults will stop.
summary: Removing disruptive influences leads to peace and harmony.

text:
He who loves purity of heart and speaks gracefully
is the king’s friend.
summary: Those who maintain a pure heart and speak kindly gain favor and friendship with leaders.

text:
Yahweh’s eyes watch over knowledge;
but he frustrates the words of the unfaithful.
summary: God values true knowledge and watches over it, but he thwarts the plans of those who are unfaithful.

text:
The sluggard says, “There is a lion outside!
I will be killed in the streets!”
summary: Excuses often mask laziness and prevent us from facing challenges responsibly.

text:
The mouth of an adulteress is a deep pit.
He who is under Yahweh’s wrath will fall into it.
summary: Engaging with unfaithfulness leads to serious consequences and divine disfavor.

text:
Folly is bound up in the heart of a child:
the rod of discipline drives it far from him.
summary: Children naturally have foolish tendencies, but consistent discipline helps guide them away from harmful behavior.

text:
Whoever oppresses the poor for his own increase and whoever gives to the rich,
both come to poverty.
summary: Exploiting the poor for personal gain or favoring the rich leads to ruin for both parties.

text:
Turn your ear, and listen to the words of the wise.
Apply your heart to my teaching.
summary: Pay close attention and sincerely apply the teachings of the wise to your life.

text:
For it is a pleasant thing if you keep them within you,
if all of them are ready on your lips.
summary: It's beneficial to internalize wise teachings so you can readily share them when needed.

text:
I teach you today, even you,
So that your trust may be in Yahweh.
summary: Teaching and guidance are given to encourage trust in God.

text:
Haven’t I written to you thirty excellent things
of counsel and knowledge,
summary: I have shared many valuable pieces of advice and knowledge with you.

text:
To teach you truth, reliable words,
to give sound answers to the ones who sent you?
summary: Providing truthful and reliable guidance ensures sound answers for those seeking help.

text:
Don’t exploit the poor, because he is poor;
and don’t crush the needy in court;
summary: Treat the poor and needy with fairness and justice, avoiding exploitation or oppression.

text:
for Yahweh will plead their case,
and plunder the life of those who plunder them.
summary: God defends the oppressed and punishes those who exploit them.

text:
Don’t befriend a hot-tempered man,
and don’t associate with one who harbors anger:
summary: Avoid close relationships with people who are quick to anger to maintain your own peace and well-being.

text:
lest you learn his ways,
and ensnare your soul.
summary: Avoid getting involved with harmful people to protect your well-being.

text:
Don’t you be one of those who strike hands,
of those who are collateral for debts.
summary: Avoid making risky financial commitments by guaranteeing others' debts to protect yourself from potential loss.

text:
If you don’t have means to pay,
why should he take away your bed from under you?
summary: Don't put up collateral you can't afford to lose, or you risk losing even your basic necessities.

text:
Don’t move the ancient boundary stone,
which your fathers have set up.
summary: Respect established boundaries and traditions to maintain order and fairness.

text:
Do you see a man skilled in his work?
He will serve kings.
He won’t serve obscure men.
summary: A person who is highly skilled and diligent in their work will gain recognition and serve influential leaders rather than unknown individuals.

text:
When you sit to eat with a ruler,
consider diligently what is before you;
summary: Be careful and thoughtful about what you consume and how you behave when dining with someone in authority.

text:
put a knife to your throat,
if you are a man given to appetite.
summary: Exercise strict self-control to avoid overindulgence in your desires.

text:
Don’t be desirous of his dainties,
since they are deceitful food.
summary: Don't crave tempting things that can ultimately harm or deceive you.

text:
Don’t weary yourself to be rich.
In your wisdom, show restraint.
summary: Don't exhaust yourself trying to get rich; instead, exercise wise restraint and balance in your pursuits.

text:
Why do you set your eyes on that which is not?
For it certainly sprouts wings like an eagle and flies in the sky.
summary: Don't fixate on wealth or possessions, as they are fleeting and can disappear quickly.

text:
Don’t eat the food of him who has a stingy eye,
and don’t crave his delicacies:
summary: Avoid accepting generosity from those who are selfish or begrudging, as their motives may be harmful.

text:
for as he thinks about the cost, so he is.
“Eat and drink!” he says to you,
but his heart is not with you.
summary: A person's true intentions are revealed by their thoughts, not just their words or actions.

text:
The morsel which you have eaten you shall vomit up,
and lose your good words.
summary: If you accept a small favor or gift, you might lose your good reputation if you later reject or betray the giver.

text:
Don’t speak in the ears of a fool,
for he will despise the wisdom of your words.
summary: Avoid sharing your wisdom with those who won't appreciate it, as they may reject or disrespect your advice.

text:
Don’t move the ancient boundary stone.
Don’t encroach on the fields of the fatherless:
summary: Respect established boundaries and protect the rights of the vulnerable, like orphans.

text:
for their Defender is strong.
He will plead their case against you.
summary: Those who are vulnerable have a powerful advocate who will defend their rights against injustice.

text:
Apply your heart to instruction,
and your ears to the words of knowledge.
summary: Focus your attention and eagerness on learning and gaining knowledge.

text:
Don’t withhold correction from a child.
If you punish him with the rod, he will not die.
summary: Consistent correction and discipline help guide children safely and wisely through life.

text:
Punish him with the rod,
and save his soul from Sheol.
summary: Correcting a child firmly can protect them from serious harm or ruin later in life.

text:
My son, if your heart is wise,
then my heart will be glad, even mine:
summary: A parent's joy comes from seeing their child grow in wisdom and understanding.

text:
yes, my heart will rejoice,
when your lips speak what is right.
summary: True joy comes when others speak honestly and rightly.

text:
Don’t let your heart envy sinners;
but rather fear Yahweh all day long.
summary: Avoid envying those who do wrong; instead, maintain a reverent respect for God consistently.

text:
Indeed surely there is a future hope,
and your hope will not be cut off.
summary: Trust that your future holds promise and your hopes will not be disappointed.

text:
Listen, my son, and be wise,
and keep your heart on the right path!
summary: Pay attention and choose to keep your inner self aligned with what is right.

text:
Don’t be among ones drinking too much wine,
or those who gorge themselves on meat:
summary: Avoid excessive indulgence in food and drink to maintain self-control and well-being.

text:
for the drunkard and the glutton shall become poor;
and drowsiness clothes them in rags.
summary: Excessive drinking, overeating, and laziness lead to poverty and ruin.

text:
Listen to your father who gave you life,
and don’t despise your mother when she is old.
summary: Respect and honor your parents throughout their lives, especially as they grow old.

text:
Buy the truth, and don’t sell it.
Get wisdom, discipline, and understanding.
summary: Value truth and invest in gaining wisdom, discipline, and understanding above all else.

text:
The father of the righteous has great joy.
Whoever fathers a wise child delights in him.
summary: Parents find deep joy and pride in raising wise and righteous children.

text:
Let your father and your mother be glad!
Let her who bore you rejoice!
summary: Bring joy to your parents by living in a way that honors and pleases them.

text:
My son, give me your heart;
and let your eyes keep in my ways.
summary: Give your full loyalty and attention to guidance and follow the right path.

text:
For a prostitute is a deep pit;
and a wayward wife is a narrow well.
summary: Engaging in immoral relationships can lead to dangerous and difficult situations.

text:
Yes, she lies in wait like a robber,
and increases the unfaithful among men.
summary: Beware of those who deceive and lead others into unfaithfulness, as they cause harm and betrayal.

text:
Who has woe?
Who has sorrow?
Who has strife?
Who has complaints?
Who has needless bruises?
Who has bloodshot eyes?
summary: Excessive indulgence leads to pain, conflict, and regret.

text:
Those who stay long at the wine;
those who go to seek out mixed wine.
summary: Spending too much time drinking alcohol leads to trouble and poor decisions.

text:
Don’t look at the wine when it is red,
when it sparkles in the cup,
when it goes down smoothly.
summary: Avoid being enticed by the appealing appearance and smoothness of wine, as it can lead to poor decisions.

text:
In the end, it bites like a snake,
and poisons like a viper.
summary: What seems harmless at first can ultimately cause great harm, so be cautious and disciplined in your choices.

text:
Your eyes will see strange things,
and your mind will imagine confusing things.
summary: Overindulgence or lack of self-control can lead to confusion and distorted perceptions.

text:
Yes, you will be as he who lies down in the middle of the sea,
or as he who lies on top of the rigging:
summary: You will feel helpless and exposed, like being stranded in a dangerous place without control.

text:
“They hit me, and I was not hurt!
They beat me, and I don’t feel it!
When will I wake up? I can do it again.
I can find another.”
summary: Enduring hardship without feeling pain can lead to repeated mistakes or harmful behavior.

text:
Don’t be envious of evil men;
neither desire to be with them:
summary: Avoid envying or associating with those who do wrong to maintain your own moral character.

text:
for their hearts plot violence,
and their lips talk about mischief.
summary: Some people secretly plan harm and openly speak of trouble.

text:
Through wisdom a house is built;
by understanding it is established;
summary: A home is created and made strong through wisdom and understanding.

text:
by knowledge the rooms are filled
with all rare and beautiful treasure.
summary: Knowledge enriches your life and surroundings with valuable and beautiful things.

text:
A wise man has great power;
and a knowledgeable man increases strength;
summary: A wise and knowledgeable person gains influence and strength through their understanding.

text:
for by wise guidance you wage your war;
and victory is in many advisors.
summary: Success comes from seeking and following wise counsel and advice from many advisors.

text:
Wisdom is too high for a fool:
he doesn’t open his mouth in the gate.
summary: Fools lack the insight to understand wisdom and therefore remain silent in important discussions.

text:
One who plots to do evil
will be called a schemer.
summary: People who plan to do harm will be recognized as deceitful schemers.

text:
The schemes of folly are sin.
The mocker is detested by men.
summary: Foolish plans are wrong, and people dislike those who mock others.

text:
If you falter in the time of trouble,
your strength is small.
summary: When difficulties arise, your true strength is revealed by how steadfast you remain.

text:
Rescue those who are being led away to death!
Indeed, hold back those who are staggering to the slaughter!
summary: Actively intervene to save others from harm or destruction whenever you can.

text:
If you say, “Behold, we didn’t know this”;
doesn’t he who weighs the hearts consider it?
He who keeps your soul, doesn’t he know it?
Shall he not render to every man according to his work?
summary: One cannot hide their true intentions or actions from God, who judges everyone fairly based on their deeds.

text:
My son, eat honey, for it is good;
the droppings of the honeycomb, which are sweet to your taste:
summary: Enjoy the good and sweet things in life, as they provide nourishment and satisfaction.
Gaining wisdom nourishes your soul and brings lasting hope and reward.

text:
Don’t lay in wait, wicked man, against the habitation of the righteous.
Don’t destroy his resting place:
summary: Avoid plotting harm against those who live righteously and respect their peace.

text:
for a righteous man falls seven times, and rises up again;
but the wicked are overthrown by calamity.
summary: A righteous person may face repeated failures but perseveres, while the wicked are ultimately destroyed by their troubles.

text:
Don’t rejoice when your enemy falls.
Don’t let your heart be glad when he is overthrown;
summary: Don't take pleasure in the misfortune of your enemies; maintain compassion and integrity even toward those who oppose you.

text:
lest Yahweh see it, and it displease him,
and he turn away his wrath from him.
summary: Avoid actions that might displease God and bring His wrath upon you.

text:
Don’t fret yourself because of evildoers;
neither be envious of the wicked:
summary: Don't waste your energy worrying about or envying those who do wrong.

text:
for there will be no reward to the evil man;
and the lamp of the wicked shall be snuffed out.
summary: Evil actions ultimately lead to ruin and loss of reward.

text:
My son, fear Yahweh and the king.
Don’t join those who are rebellious:
summary: Respect authority and avoid associating with rebels to maintain order and safety.

text:
for their calamity will rise suddenly;
the destruction from them both—who knows?
summary: Unexpected disaster can come suddenly, so be cautious and prepared.

text:
These also are sayings of the wise.
To show partiality in judgment is not good.
summary: Fair and impartial judgment is essential for true wisdom and justice.

text:
He who says to the wicked, “You are righteous”;
peoples will curse him, and nations will abhor him—
summary: If you praise or support the wicked as if they are righteous, you will lose the respect of others and be condemned by society.

text:
but it will go well with those who convict the guilty,
and a rich blessing will come on them.
summary: Those who stand up for justice and hold wrongdoers accountable will be rewarded and blessed.

text:
An honest answer
is like a kiss on the lips.
summary: Giving a truthful and sincere response is as valuable and pleasant as a loving kiss.

text:
Prepare your work outside,
and get your fields ready.
Afterwards, build your house.
summary: Plan and complete your essential tasks first before focusing on personal comforts.

text:
Don’t be a witness against your neighbor without cause.
Don’t deceive with your lips.
summary: Avoid giving false testimony or speaking deceitfully against others without reason.

text:
Don’t say, “I will do to him as he has done to me;
I will render to the man according to his work.”
summary: Avoid seeking personal revenge and instead act with fairness and integrity.

text:
I went by the field of the sluggard,
by the vineyard of the man void of understanding;
summary: Observing the neglected field of a lazy person reveals the consequences of lack of effort.

text:
Behold, it was all grown over with thorns.
Its surface was covered with nettles,
and its stone wall was broken down.
summary: Neglected tasks or responsibilities will become overrun with problems and decay over time.

text:
Then I saw, and considered well.
I saw, and received instruction:
summary: Careful observation and reflection lead to valuable learning and insight.

text:
a little sleep, a little slumber,
a little folding of the hands to sleep;
summary: A bit of laziness or rest can lead to neglecting important tasks.

text:
so your poverty will come as a robber,
and your want as an armed man.
summary: Neglecting your responsibilities can lead to sudden and severe hardship, like a thief or an armed attacker catching you off guard.

text: These also are proverbs of Solomon, which the men of Hezekiah king of Judah copied out.
summary: This is an introduction noting that these proverbs were recorded during King Hezekiah's time.

text:
It is the glory of God to conceal a thing,
but the glory of kings is to search out a matter.
summary: It is honorable to seek understanding and uncover hidden truths, reflecting both divine mystery and human diligence.

text:
As the heavens for height, and the earth for depth,
so the hearts of kings are unsearchable.
summary: Just as the vastness of the sky and earth cannot be fully measured, the thoughts and intentions of rulers are often beyond our understanding.

text:
Take away the dross from the silver,
and material comes out for the refiner;
summary: Removing impurities reveals true value, just as refining removes dross from silver.

text:
Take away the wicked from the king’s presence,
and his throne will be established in righteousness.
summary: Removing corrupt influences from leadership helps establish a just and stable rule.

text:
Don’t exalt yourself in the presence of the king,
or claim a place among great men;
summary: Avoid boasting or seeking attention in front of those in authority to maintain respect and favor.

text:
for it is better that it be said to you, “Come up here,”
than that you should be put lower in the presence of the prince,
whom your eyes have seen.
summary: It's better to be invited to a higher position than to be embarrassed by being placed lower in front of important people.

text:
Don’t be hasty in bringing charges to court.
What will you do in the end when your neighbor shames you?
summary: Think carefully before accusing someone, as rushing to judgment can lead to personal embarrassment.

text:
Debate your case with your neighbor,
and don’t betray the confidence of another;
summary: Address disputes directly with those involved while respecting others' confidences.

text:
lest one who hears it put you to shame,
and your bad reputation never depart.
summary: Avoid actions that could cause others to embarrass you and damage your reputation permanently.

text:
A word fitly spoken
is like apples of gold in settings of silver.
summary: Well-timed and appropriate words are as valuable and beautiful as golden apples in silver settings.

text:
As an earring of gold, and an ornament of fine gold,
so is a wise reprover to an obedient ear.
summary: A wise correction is as valuable and beautiful as fine gold to someone willing to listen.
A reliable and trustworthy messenger brings much-needed relief and encouragement to those who depend on him.

text:
As clouds and wind without rain,
so is he who boasts of gifts deceptively.
summary: Empty promises or boastful claims without real generosity are disappointing and unreliable.

text:
By patience a ruler is persuaded.
A soft tongue breaks the bone.
summary: Gentle and patient words can influence even the most powerful people effectively.

text:
Have you found honey?
Eat as much as is sufficient for you,
lest you eat too much, and vomit it.
summary: Enjoy good things in moderation to avoid negative consequences.

text:
Let your foot be seldom in your neighbor’s house,
lest he be weary of you, and hate you.
summary: Don't overstay your welcome with others to maintain good relationships.

text:
A man who gives false testimony against his neighbor
is like a club, a sword, or a sharp arrow.
summary: Giving false testimony harms others like a weapon causes injury.

text:
Confidence in someone unfaithful in time of trouble
is like a bad tooth, or a lame foot.
summary: Relying on an untrustworthy person during difficult times is as painful and unreliable as a bad tooth or a lame foot.

text:
As one who takes away a garment in cold weather,
or vinegar on soda,
so is one who sings songs to a heavy heart.
summary: Offering inappropriate comfort or cheer to someone who is grieving can make things worse, much like removing a coat in cold weather or mixing vinegar with soda.

text:
If your enemy is hungry, give him food to eat.
If he is thirsty, give him water to drink:
summary: Respond to your enemies with kindness and generosity to overcome hostility and promote peace.

text:
for you will heap coals of fire on his head,
and Yahweh will reward you.
summary: Responding to your enemies with kindness can lead to their remorse and bring you divine reward.

text:
The north wind produces rain;
so a backbiting tongue brings an angry face.
summary: Just as the north wind brings rain, gossip and backbiting lead to conflict and anger.

text:
Like cold water to a thirsty soul,
so is good news from a far country.
summary: Receiving good news from afar refreshes and uplifts the spirit like cold water refreshes someone who is thirsty.

text:
Like a muddied spring, and a polluted well,
so is a righteous man who gives way before the wicked.
summary: A righteous person who yields to the wicked loses their purity and moral standing, much like clean water becoming contaminated.

text:
It is not good to eat much honey;
nor is it honorable to seek one’s own honor.
summary: Excessive indulgence and selfish pursuit of personal glory are both unwise and dishonorable.

text:
Like a city that is broken down and without walls
is a man whose spirit is without restraint.
summary: A person without self-control is vulnerable, like a defenseless city without walls.

text:
Like snow in summer, and as rain in harvest,
so honor is not fitting for a fool.
summary: Giving honor to a fool is as inappropriate and out of place as snow in summer or rain during harvest.

text:
Like a fluttering sparrow,
like a darting swallow,
so the undeserved curse doesn’t come to rest.
summary: An undeserved curse or accusation will not have lasting effect or harm on a person of integrity.

text:
A whip is for the horse,
a bridle for the donkey,
and a rod for the back of fools!
summary: Just as animals need tools to guide and correct them, fools require firm discipline to learn and improve.

text:
Don’t answer a fool according to his folly,
lest you also be like him.
summary: Avoid responding to foolishness in a foolish way, so you don't become like the fool yourself.

text:
Answer a fool according to his folly,
lest he be wise in his own eyes.
summary: Sometimes you need to respond to foolishness in a way that exposes it, so the fool doesn't become conceited.

text:
One who sends a message by the hand of a fool
is cutting off feet and drinking violence.
summary: Entrusting important messages to a foolish person can cause harm and escalate conflict.

text:
Like the legs of the lame that hang loose,
so is a parable in the mouth of fools.
summary: Just as a lame person's legs are useless, a parable told by a fool lacks effectiveness and meaning.

text:
As one who binds a stone in a sling,
so is he who gives honor to a fool.
summary: Giving honor or respect to a fool is as unwise and dangerous as putting a stone in a sling—it can backfire and cause harm.

text:
Like a thorn bush that goes into the hand of a drunkard,
so is a parable in the mouth of fools.
summary: A wise saying is wasted or harmful when spoken by someone who lacks understanding.

text:
As an archer who wounds all,
so is he who hires a fool
or he who hires those who pass by.
summary: Hiring foolish or unreliable people can cause widespread harm, much like an archer who injures everyone in sight.

text:
As a dog that returns to his vomit,
so is a fool who repeats his folly.
summary: A fool who keeps making the same mistakes is like a dog that returns to its vomit—it's unwise and harmful behavior.

text:
Do you see a man wise in his own eyes?
There is more hope for a fool than for him.
summary: Overconfidence in one's own wisdom is more dangerous than foolishness, so humility is essential.

text:
The sluggard says, “There is a lion in the road!
A fierce lion roams the streets!”
summary: A lazy person makes excuses to avoid work, exaggerating dangers to justify inactivity.

text:
As the door turns on its hinges,
so does the sluggard on his bed.
summary: Just as a door swings back and forth on its hinges without going anywhere, a lazy person turns over in bed without making progress.

text:
The sluggard buries his hand in the dish.
He is too lazy to bring it back to his mouth.
summary: A lazy person avoids even the simplest effort to satisfy their needs.

text:
The sluggard is wiser in his own eyes
than seven men who answer with discretion.
summary: A lazy person often overestimates their own wisdom compared to those who are truly prudent and thoughtful.

text:
Like one who grabs a dog’s ears
is one who passes by and meddles in a quarrel not his own.
summary: Interfering in conflicts that don't involve you often leads to trouble and is unwise.

text: Like a madman who shoots torches, arrows, and death,
summary: A person acting recklessly or irrationally causes harm without thinking about the consequences.

text: is the man who deceives his neighbor and says, “Am I not joking?”
summary: Deceiving others and then dismissing it as a joke shows a lack of honesty and responsibility.

text:
For lack of wood a fire goes out.
Without gossip, a quarrel dies down.
summary: Just as a fire needs wood to keep burning, conflicts need gossip to continue; without it, they will fade away.

text:
As coals are to hot embers,
and wood to fire,
so is a contentious man to kindling strife.
summary: A quarrelsome person is like fuel that intensifies conflict and causes disputes to flare up.

text:
The words of a whisperer are as dainty morsels,
they go down into the innermost parts.
summary: Gossip and whispered words can deeply influence and affect people, much like tasty food satisfies the body.

text:
Like silver dross on an earthen vessel
are the lips of a fervent one with an evil heart.
summary: Insincere or harmful words from a passionate person are like worthless impurities on a valuable container.

text:
A malicious man disguises himself with his lips,
but he harbors evil in his heart.
summary: A person may speak kindly but still conceal harmful intentions within.

text:
When his speech is charming, don’t believe him;
for there are seven abominations in his heart.
summary: Don't be deceived by flattering words, as they can hide harmful intentions.

text:
His malice may be concealed by deception,
but his wickedness will be exposed in the assembly.
summary: Hidden evil intentions will eventually be revealed publicly, exposing the wrongdoer.

text:
Whoever digs a pit shall fall into it.
Whoever rolls a stone, it will come back on him.
summary: Those who plot harm against others will ultimately suffer the consequences themselves.

text:
A lying tongue hates those it hurts;
and a flattering mouth works ruin.
summary: Deceptive and flattering words can harm others and lead to destruction.

text:
Don’t boast about tomorrow;
for you don’t know what a day may bring.
summary: Don't brag about future plans because the future is uncertain and beyond your control.
It's better to let others praise you than to boast about yourself.
Dealing with a fool's insults or provocations is more burdensome than physical hardships like carrying heavy stones or sand.
While anger and wrath are harsh and overpowering emotions, jealousy is even more difficult to endure or confront.

text:
Better is open rebuke
than hidden love.
summary: It's better to be honestly corrected than to have love that is kept secret and unexpressed.

text:
Faithful are the wounds of a friend;
although the kisses of an enemy are profuse.
summary: True friends may offer honest criticism that hurts, while insincere enemies offer flattering words.

text:
A full soul loathes a honeycomb;
but to a hungry soul, every bitter thing is sweet.
summary: Contentment changes our perspective, making what we have seem less appealing when satisfied, but even hardships feel valuable when we are in need.

text:
As a bird that wanders from her nest,
so is a man who wanders from his home.
summary: Just as a bird feels unsettled when away from its nest, a person feels uneasy when separated from their home and loved ones.

text:
Perfume and incense bring joy to the heart;
so does earnest counsel from a man’s friend.
summary: Just as pleasant scents delight the senses, sincere advice from a true friend brings joy and comfort to the heart.

text:
Don’t forsake your friend and your father’s friend.
Don’t go to your brother’s house in the day of your disaster:
better is a neighbor who is near than a distant brother.
summary: In times of trouble, close friends and nearby neighbors can be more reliable than distant family, so value and maintain those close bonds.

text:
Be wise, my son,
and bring joy to my heart,
then I can answer my tormentor.
summary: Act wisely to bring happiness to those who care for you, strengthening your position against critics.

text:
A prudent man sees danger and takes refuge;
but the simple pass on, and suffer for it.
summary: A wise person anticipates trouble and avoids it, while a naive person ignores warnings and faces harm.

text:
Take his garment when he puts up collateral for a stranger.
Hold it for a wayward woman!
summary: Be cautious and hold someone accountable when they guarantee a stranger's debt or commitment.

text:
He who blesses his neighbor with a loud voice early in the morning,
it will be taken as a curse by him.
summary: Offering good wishes too loudly or at an inconvenient time can annoy others instead of pleasing them.

text:
A continual dropping on a rainy day
and a contentious wife are alike:
summary: Constant nagging or conflict can be as irritating and persistent as continuous dripping rain.

text:
restraining her is like restraining the wind,
or like grasping oil in his right hand.
summary: Trying to control someone who is naturally free-spirited is as difficult as holding the wind or slippery oil.

text:
Iron sharpens iron;
so a man sharpens his friend’s countenance.
summary: Just as iron sharpens iron, friends improve and encourage each other through honest interaction.

text:
Whoever tends the fig tree shall eat its fruit.
He who looks after his master shall be honored.
summary: Taking care of your responsibilities leads to personal benefit and respect from others.

text:
Like water reflects a face,
so a man’s heart reflects the man.
summary: Just as water shows a clear reflection of a face, a person's true character is revealed by their heart.

text:
Sheol and Abaddon are never satisfied;
and a man’s eyes are never satisfied.
summary: Just as the grave is never full, human desire is insatiable and always seeking more.
Just as precious metals are tested by fire, a person's true character is revealed through how they handle praise.

text:
Though you grind a fool in a mortar with a pestle along with grain,
yet his foolishness will not be removed from him.
summary: No matter how much you try to correct a fool, their foolishness remains unchanged.

text:
Know well the state of your flocks,
and pay attention to your herds:
summary: Be attentive and careful in managing your responsibilities and resources.

text:
for riches are not forever,
nor does the crown endure to all generations.
summary: Wealth and power are temporary and do not last forever across generations.

text:
The hay is removed, and the new growth appears,
the grasses of the hills are gathered in.
summary: After one harvest is gathered, new growth will come, reminding us of the ongoing cycles of work and renewal.

text:
The lambs are for your clothing,
and the goats are the price of a field.
summary: Livestock can provide valuable resources like clothing and property, highlighting practical uses of possessions.
Having abundant resources ensures that everyone in your household is well cared for.

text:
The wicked flee when no one pursues;
but the righteous are as bold as a lion.
summary: A person with integrity acts confidently and without fear, while the guilty are anxious even without cause.

text:
In rebellion, a land has many rulers,
but order is maintained by a man of understanding and knowledge.
summary: A nation with many leaders due to rebellion lacks stability, but wise and knowledgeable leadership brings order.

text:
A needy man who oppresses the poor
is like a driving rain which leaves no crops.
summary: A poor person who exploits others causes harm to everyone, just like relentless rain ruins crops.

text:
Those who forsake the law praise the wicked;
but those who keep the law contend with them.
summary: People who abandon moral principles end up supporting wrongdoing, while those who uphold the law stand against it.

text:
Evil men don’t understand justice;
but those who seek Yahweh understand it fully.
summary: Those who pursue righteousness and seek God gain true understanding of justice, unlike the wicked who fail to grasp it.

text:
Better is the poor who walks in his integrity,
than he who is perverse in his ways, and he is rich.
summary: It's better to be poor and honest than wealthy through dishonest means.

text:
Whoever keeps the law is a wise son;
but he who is a companion of gluttons shames his father.
summary: Respecting rules shows wisdom, but associating with reckless people brings shame to your family.

text:
He who increases his wealth by excessive interest
gathers it for one who has pity on the poor.
summary: Gaining wealth through unfair interest ultimately benefits those who show compassion to the poor.

text:
He who turns away his ear from hearing the law,
even his prayer is an abomination.
summary: Ignoring guidance and moral instruction makes one's prayers insincere and unacceptable.
Leading honest people into wrongdoing will backfire on you, while those who remain blameless will be rewarded.

text:
The rich man is wise in his own eyes;
but the poor who has understanding sees through him.
summary: Wealth can give a false sense of wisdom, but true understanding comes from insight, not riches.

text:
When the righteous triumph, there is great glory;
but when the wicked rise, men hide themselves.
summary: When good people succeed, it brings honor, but when evil people gain power, others fear and avoid them.

text:
He who conceals his sins doesn’t prosper,
but whoever confesses and renounces them finds mercy.
summary: Hiding your mistakes hinders your growth, but admitting and turning away from them leads to forgiveness and success.

text:
Blessed is the man who always fears;
but one who hardens his heart falls into trouble.
summary: A person who remains cautious and reverent avoids trouble, while stubbornness leads to problems.

text:
As a roaring lion or a charging bear,
so is a wicked ruler over helpless people.
summary: A corrupt leader is as dangerous and oppressive to the vulnerable as a fierce wild animal.

text:
A tyrannical ruler lacks judgment.
One who hates ill-gotten gain will have long days.
summary: A leader who rules unjustly lacks wisdom, but those who reject dishonest gain will enjoy a long life.

text:
A man who is tormented by life blood will be a fugitive until death;
no one will support him.
summary: A person who causes serious harm to others will live in fear and isolation without support.

text:
Whoever walks blamelessly is kept safe;
but one with perverse ways will fall suddenly.
summary: Living with integrity provides security, while crooked behavior leads to sudden downfall.

text:
One who works his land will have an abundance of food;
but one who chases fantasies will have his fill of poverty.
summary: Hard work and practical effort lead to abundance, while pursuing unrealistic dreams leads to lack.

text:
A faithful man is rich with blessings;
but one who is eager to be rich will not go unpunished.
summary: Being faithful and trustworthy brings true blessings, while greed for wealth leads to trouble.

text:
To show partiality is not good;
yet a man will do wrong for a piece of bread.
summary: Favoring some unfairly is wrong, but people may compromise their integrity when desperate.

text:
A stingy man hurries after riches,
and doesn’t know that poverty waits for him.
summary: Chasing wealth greedily can ironically lead to poverty.

text:
One who rebukes a man will afterward find more favor
than one who flatters with the tongue.
summary: Honest correction earns more respect than insincere flattery.

text:
Whoever robs his father or his mother, and says, “It’s not wrong.”
He is a partner with a destroyer.
summary: Stealing from your parents and denying it's wrong aligns you with those who cause harm and destruction.

text:
One who is greedy stirs up strife;
but one who trusts in Yahweh will prosper.
summary: Greed causes conflict, but trusting in God leads to true prosperity.

text:
One who trusts in himself is a fool;
but one who walks in wisdom is kept safe.
summary: Relying solely on your own understanding is unwise, but following wisdom leads to safety and security.

text:
One who gives to the poor has no lack;
but one who closes his eyes will have many curses.
summary: Generosity towards the poor leads to abundance, while ignoring their needs brings misfortune.

text:
When the wicked rise, men hide themselves;
but when they perish, the righteous thrive.
summary: When evil people gain power, others live in fear, but when they fall, good people prosper.

text:
He who is often rebuked and stiffens his neck
will be destroyed suddenly, with no remedy.
summary: Ignoring correction and refusing to change leads to sudden and irreversible consequences.

text:
When the righteous thrive, the people rejoice;
but when the wicked rule, the people groan.
summary: Good leadership by righteous people brings joy to society, while wicked rulers cause suffering.

text:
Whoever loves wisdom brings joy to his father;
but a companion of prostitutes squanders his wealth.
summary: Valuing wisdom pleases your family, while bad company leads to financial ruin.

text:
The king by justice makes the land stable,
but he who takes bribes tears it down.
summary: A just leader brings stability to a nation, while corruption leads to its downfall.

text:
A man who flatters his neighbor
spreads a net for his feet.
summary: Flattering others insincerely can lead to trouble and harm your own well-being.

text:
An evil man is snared by his sin,
but the righteous can sing and be glad.
summary: Those who do wrong are trapped by their own actions, while the righteous find joy and freedom.

text:
The righteous care about justice for the poor.
The wicked aren’t concerned about knowledge.
summary: Righteous people prioritize justice for the poor, while the wicked ignore the value of knowledge.

text:
Mockers stir up a city,
but wise men turn away anger.
summary: Fools provoke conflict and unrest, while the wise work to calm and resolve anger.

text:
If a wise man goes to court with a foolish man,
the fool rages or scoffs, and there is no peace.
summary: When a wise person deals with a foolish one, conflict and unrest often arise due to the fool's anger or mockery.

text:
The bloodthirsty hate a man of integrity;
and they seek the life of the upright.
summary: People with harmful intentions often oppose those who live honestly and seek to harm them.

text:
A fool vents all of his anger,
but a wise man brings himself under control.
summary: A wise person controls their anger, while a fool lets it all out impulsively.

text:
If a ruler listens to lies,
all of his officials are wicked.
summary: When leaders accept falsehoods, it leads to widespread corruption among their subordinates.

text:
The poor man and the oppressor have this in common:
Yahweh gives sight to the eyes of both.
summary: God provides insight and awareness to everyone, regardless of their social status or behavior.

text:
The king who fairly judges the poor,
his throne shall be established forever.
summary: A leader who judges the poor with fairness will secure a lasting and stable reign.

text:
The rod of correction gives wisdom,
but a child left to himself causes shame to his mother.
summary: Proper correction helps a child gain wisdom, while neglecting discipline leads to problems and shame.

text:
When the wicked increase, sin increases;
but the righteous will see their downfall.
summary: As wickedness grows, sin spreads, but eventually the righteous will witness the downfall of the wicked.

text:
Correct your son, and he will give you peace;
yes, he will bring delight to your soul.
summary: Guiding and correcting your child leads to peace and joy in your life.

text:
Where there is no revelation, the people cast off restraint;
but one who keeps the law is blessed.
summary: Without clear guidance or vision, people lose self-control, but following principles brings blessings.

text:
A servant can’t be corrected by words.
Though he understands, yet he will not respond.
summary: Some people may understand advice or correction but choose not to change their behavior.

text:
Do you see a man who is hasty in his words?
There is more hope for a fool than for him.
summary: Being quick to speak without thinking is more harmful than foolishness, so it's better to be slow and thoughtful in what you say.

text:
He who pampers his servant from youth
will have him become a son in the end.
summary: Treating those under your care kindly and generously from the start can build deep, lasting bonds like family.

text:
An angry man stirs up strife,
and a wrathful man abounds in sin.
summary: A person who often gets angry causes conflicts and commits many wrongs.

text:
A man’s pride brings him low,
but one of lowly spirit gains honor.
summary: Pride leads to downfall, but humility brings respect and honor.

text:
Whoever is an accomplice of a thief is an enemy of his own soul.
He takes an oath, but dares not testify.
summary: Supporting wrongdoing harms yourself and shows a lack of true commitment to honesty.

text:
The fear of man proves to be a snare,
but whoever puts his trust in Yahweh is kept safe.
summary: Worrying about others' opinions traps you, but trusting in God keeps you secure.

text:
Many seek the ruler’s favor,
but a man’s justice comes from Yahweh.
summary: While many try to gain the approval of leaders, true justice and righteousness come from God.

text:
A dishonest man detests the righteous,
and the upright in their ways detest the wicked.
summary: Dishonest people dislike those who live rightly, and those who live rightly dislike the wicked.

text:
The words of Agur the son of Jakeh; the revelation:
the man says to Ithiel,
to Ithiel and Ucal:
summary: This is an introduction to a saying, without clear wisdom or guidance on its own.

text:
“Surely I am the most ignorant man,
and don’t have a man’s understanding.
summary: Recognizing and admitting one's own ignorance is a wise step toward gaining true understanding.

text:
I have not learned wisdom,
neither do I have the knowledge of the Holy One.
summary: Acknowledging one's own lack of wisdom and knowledge is a humble and honest stance.

text:
Who has ascended up into heaven, and descended?
Who has gathered the wind in his fists?
Who has bound the waters in his garment?
Who has established all the ends of the earth?
What is his name, and what is his son’s name, if you know?
summary: True wisdom recognizes the greatness and mystery of the divine, beyond human understanding.

text:
“Every word of God is flawless.
He is a shield to those who take refuge in him.
summary: God's words are perfect and provide protection to those who trust in Him.

text:
Don’t you add to his words,
lest he reprove you, and you be found a liar.
summary: Don't distort or add to truthful words, or you risk being corrected and exposed as dishonest.

text:
“Two things I have asked of you;
don’t deny me before I die:
summary: The speaker humbly requests two essential things from God, seeking His favor before death.
Ask for honesty and a balanced life without extreme wealth or poverty, receiving only what is truly necessary.

text:
lest I be full, deny you, and say, ‘Who is Yahweh?’
or lest I be poor, and steal,
and so dishonor the name of my God.
summary: Avoiding both excessive wealth and poverty helps maintain faithfulness and prevents dishonoring God through pride or wrongdoing.

text:
“Don’t slander a servant to his master,
lest he curse you, and you be held guilty.
summary: Avoid speaking ill of those under authority to their superiors, as it can backfire and make you culpable.

text:
There is a generation that curses their father,
and doesn’t bless their mother.
summary: Some people disrespect their parents instead of honoring them, which harms family bonds.

text:
There is a generation that is pure in their own eyes,
yet are not washed from their filthiness.
summary: Some people think they are morally pure, but they fail to recognize their own faults and need for cleansing.

text:
There is a generation, oh how lofty are their eyes!
Their eyelids are lifted up.
summary: Some people are proud and arrogant, looking down on others with haughty eyes.

text:
There is a generation whose teeth are like swords,
and their jaws like knives,
to devour the poor from the earth, and the needy from among men.
summary: Some people are ruthless and exploit the vulnerable for their own gain.

text:
“The leach has two daughters:
‘Give, give.’
“There are three things that are never satisfied;
four that don’t say, ‘Enough:’
summary: Some desires or needs are insatiable and constantly demand more, highlighting the challenge of self-control.
Certain things in life are insatiable and never satisfied, reminding us of the importance of recognizing limits.

text:
“The eye that mocks at his father,
and scorns obedience to his mother:
the ravens of the valley shall pick it out,
the young eagles shall eat it.
summary: Disrespecting and disobeying one's parents leads to severe consequences.

text:
“There are three things which are too amazing for me,
four which I don’t understand:
summary: Some things in life are mysterious and hard to understand.
Some things, like the mysterious ways of nature and human attraction, are difficult to understand or predict.

text:
“So is the way of an adulterous woman:
she eats and wipes her mouth,
and says, ‘I have done nothing wrong.’
summary: Some people commit wrongs but act as if they are innocent, showing a lack of accountability.

text:
“For three things the earth trembles,
and under four, it can’t bear up:
summary: There are certain serious situations that cause great instability and difficulty.

text:
For a servant when he is king;
a fool when he is filled with food;
summary: A person's character can change negatively when their circumstances improve, such as a servant becoming proud when made king or a fool becoming arrogant when well-fed.

text:
for an unloved woman when she is married;
and a servant who is heir to her mistress.
summary: An unloved woman in marriage and a servant inheriting from her mistress create uncomfortable or problematic situations.

text:
“There are four things which are little on the earth,
but they are exceedingly wise:
summary: Small creatures can possess remarkable wisdom despite their size.

text:
the ants are not a strong people,
yet they provide their food in the summer.
summary: Even though ants are small and weak, they wisely prepare and store food during the summer for future needs.

text:
The conies are but a feeble folk,
yet make they their houses in the rocks.
summary: Even those who seem weak can show great wisdom by choosing safe and secure places to live.

text:
The locusts have no king,
yet they advance in ranks.
summary: Even without a leader, organized cooperation and self-discipline can lead to effective progress.

text:
You can catch a lizard with your hands,
yet it is in kings’ palaces.
summary: Even small or humble creatures can be found in places of great power, reminding us that appearances can be deceiving.

text:
“There are three things which are stately in their march,
four which are stately in going:
summary: Some things move with a dignified and impressive manner, highlighting the beauty of purposeful movement.

text:
The lion, which is mightiest among animals,
and doesn’t turn away for any;
summary: Like the lion, be courageous and face challenges without retreating.
Certain figures, like the greyhound, the male goat, and a king who faces no rebellion, symbolize strength and authority.

text:
“If you have done foolishly in lifting up yourself,
or if you have thought evil,
put your hand over your mouth.
summary: If you have acted foolishly or entertained evil thoughts, it's wise to remain silent and not speak impulsively.
Just as physical pressure yields predictable results, provoking anger inevitably leads to conflict.

text: The words of king Lemuel; the revelation which his mother taught him.
summary: This is an introduction to advice given by King Lemuel, taught by his mother.

text:
“Oh, my son!
Oh, son of my womb!
Oh, son of my vows!
summary: A heartfelt address from a parent to their child, expressing deep personal connection.

text:
Don’t give your strength to women,
nor your ways to that which destroys kings.
summary: Avoid giving your energy and focus to things or people that can lead to your downfall.
Leaders should practice self-control and avoid indulgence in alcohol to maintain clear judgment.

text:
lest they drink, and forget the law,
and pervert the justice due to anyone who is afflicted.
summary: Avoid excessive drinking to maintain clear judgment and uphold justice for those in need.

text:
